# Thread: Mastitis
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mastitis.3890/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### trishalinn@gmail.com — 2012-09-25T03:55:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mastitis.3890/post-53986>

Alright, not sure where to put this but I decided to try here. I never had mastitis or any trouble with plugged ducts with my first two kids. But with my twinsies...yowsers, I've had it three times now! The first time was when they were just 6 weeks old. I went to my ob and she gave me an antibiotic. I had my second bout of it just 16 days ago (babiesare now 15 months). I did my research and decided to try mega doses of vit C, D, and a bunch of raw garlic (blech!) before going to the doctor and being given an antibiotic. It worked! Fever subsided, body aches went away, breast healed, all was well. Then just 3 days ago I got it in the OTHER breast! My little cocktail has once again done its magic. Still a bit of soreness in my breast, but I'm sure it will be gone by tomorrow.

SO, there are two reasons I'm writing this. 1) I wanted pass along the info in case you find yourself in a similar situation and old like to avoid antibiotics. 2) I'm wondering if there is a reason I've gotten this twice in just a couple of weeks? I'm. Assuming its simply because I have twins, who up until just about a month ago, were almost exclusively breat fed. they all of a sudden ot interested in food and drastically cut down on nursing. Anything I might be missing? I really don't want to deal with this again. It knocks me out of comission for about two days!

### Shijin13 — 2012-09-25T04:18:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mastitis.3890/post-54008>

It's the increase in solids. Nurse first then offer solids.

How many times/day are you. Nursing?

Yes the VitC, D3 & garlic does the trick every time.

### trishalinn@gmail.com — 2012-09-25T04:21:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mastitis.3890/post-54010>

I don't have a set number of times I nurse, I've always just nursed on demand. I nurse each of them probably 3-4 times during the day and still a couple times at night. The night time nursing is loooooong and drawn out. They prefer for me to not get any sleep

### freesia — 2012-09-25T04:23:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mastitis.3890/post-54013>

Just some endorsement for your cocktail here, I used massive doses of Vit C when I had mastits and duct problems and I managed to avoid antibiotics too.

I got plugged ducts each time the babies dropped demand. My midwide recommended lecithin as a preventive and it seemed to work really well...not sure exactly how. Anyway I was happy not to have to deal with those particular issues again....completely wiped me out!!

Hope you're in the clear now

# Thread: Show me your Labs: Find a Match?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/show-me-your-labs-find-a-match.3899/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Souldanzer — 2012-09-25T07:24:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/show-me-your-labs-find-a-match.3899/post-54057>

Is anyone else starting to feel like labs are some of the most personal things to share with others? Lol. I'm feeling very private about my steroid hormone panel..... I have to look at ALL the labs posted on here. I just think it's so cool and tells you so much about a person. Way more than a first date would ever tell you. I think Match.com needs to add a section for labs and hormone panels. It's like, i can see what you eat, I can see your sex drive, I can see your if you're a stressed out person, .... etc. and you can't lie either. You can write and say a lot of things about yourself... but if your T is in the toilet and your cortisol is in the sky.... I probably don't want to date you no matter how cute you might appear on your profile pic.

Maybe I'll become a matchmaker for singles who uses labs to make the matches, heehee. What do you all think?

### Paradox — 2012-09-25T08:03:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/show-me-your-labs-find-a-match.3899/post-54061>

Ohhhhh. Now that is such a good idea.

### Souldanzer — 2012-09-25T08:14:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/show-me-your-labs-find-a-match.3899/post-54063>

Immune systems..... didn't we learn recently that we tend to feel attracted to the people who have an opposing immune system for our own? So we can expand our immunity and pass on to progeny?

How do you test for immune system particulars? Then pick a boy and a girl with opposing ones... Match!  Match the sex drive, too, b/c that'll be a constant source of arguments otherwise

How about gut floras? I think those would be perfect.... we want variety. The more variety girl and boy have together the better. So maybe GENOVA and Metametrix?

I'm getting into this

### Souldanzer — 2012-09-25T08:20:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/show-me-your-labs-find-a-match.3899/post-54065>

I could create different categories for certain lab results.... then you either want opposing (immune system and gut flora) or similar (hormone panel/sex drive and ASI and food choices).

Maybe look into 23andme to put neanderthals with neanderthals... we don't want to mix species here.

Ok. I really gotta go to bed  Now what happens if one person decides to optimize and the other one doesn't?

### vkiernan — 2012-09-25T14:39:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/show-me-your-labs-find-a-match.3899/post-54085>

Soul, this is why you didn't sleep last night. This is crazy good. You are ahead of your time and I wouldn't doubt if it actually happens some day!

### vlynnb — 2012-09-25T19:40:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/show-me-your-labs-find-a-match.3899/post-54163>

Yes, Soul, your lifestyle must be optimizing your brain to come up with this.

Similar to the reasons my late family physician husband told my kids they would never be able to marry within our small community, he knew too much about every patient, even if they didn't admit to their real lifestyle.

### Souldanzer — 2012-09-26T06:17:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/show-me-your-labs-find-a-match.3899/post-54359>

gah, my brain comes up with all sorts of stuff...... I got a notebook for all the stuff I want to create and do! My problem is making it HAPPEN...... somehow I'm a bit not so useful in that department. How do you bridge the gap from creativity and imagination to reality??

Anyways. Not really planning on doing this  but I find it fascinating. Any couples wanting to submit their poop test results for preliminary data collection? I want it all, your labs, his labs..... look for patterns. If I can find about a hundred of you, I'm all set  I need an eval of how well you get along as well. Or maybe some divorced couples' labs.... now those will be kind of impossible to find.

I should better go back to trading technical analysis patterns instead of people's poop test pattern matching.........lol.

### Souldanzer — 2012-09-26T06:18:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/show-me-your-labs-find-a-match.3899/post-54361>

# Thread: CT in the South
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-in-the-south.3907/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### SueC — 2012-09-25T21:09:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-in-the-south.3907/post-54209>

I'm curious. Any of you who consider yourselves cold adapted and live in hot climes? I'm wondering if CT makes you warmer, does it also make you miserably hot is 90 degree summers? I'm a post menopausal woman who still occasionally get a hot flash or two and I'm just wondering about body heat in the wicked temps of summer. (Obviously the benefits outweight the detriments, just wondering.....)

### nonchalant — 2012-09-25T21:37:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-in-the-south.3907/post-54216>

No, actually I think I am better able to tolerate the heat now. This past summer several times I'd hear people complain, but it wasn't really bothering me. That is a complete turn-around, because I was always the first to complain.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-in-the-south.3907/post-54225>

### SueC — 2012-09-25T23:26:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-in-the-south.3907/post-54248>

Wow! Who'da thunk it? Gives me even more incentive. THANKS!

### Larry — 2012-09-25T23:30:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-in-the-south.3907/post-54249>

I know, it is pretty wild. It is certainly counter intuitive but it signals to me that the body as a whole is working much better.

### Eric — 2012-09-26T04:11:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-in-the-south.3907/post-54331>

Well, up here in PA for the summer, we were in the 90's, I would blast my A/C on full power, and the thermometer would say it was only 70'.

Even at full blast, at night I had to use thin blankets or sheets, because my trunk was radiating heat. It was nice knowing my metabolism was jacked up. It sucked cause I was like a hot coal!

Now that it's in the 50's out, and I've laid off CT for a bit (only cold showers for a week or two), it's not quite so bad at night.

### MamaGrok — 2012-09-26T05:12:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-in-the-south.3907/post-54342>

I was cold adapted before summer hit, and even though we hit the 90's in mid-May and stayed there till August this year (longest hot summer I can remember), I was far more comfortable than others seemed to be, whether working in the heat or sitting inside in excessive air conditioning.

My thermostat just works better now. It keeps me cool when it's hot out and warm when it's cold.

### Birdy — 2012-09-28T02:09:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-in-the-south.3907/post-54899>

ct'er in a hot climate and it was a cinch this summer for the most part. I did have trouble the first few weeks of ct, weird temp swings. But another thing, whenever it was super hot, I'd just head for the cold tub and that made things much more comfy for hours afterwards. I feel like I am experiencing summer just like a kid. I used to run around and do everything, whether it was "too hot" or "too cold".

### hazyjane — 2012-09-29T23:37:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-in-the-south.3907/post-55290>

I live in the South and I think I tolerate heat a bit better than I used to.

It was also fun going back up to Michigan and not being at all cold in the lakes up there (the water was warmer than usual, but I still noticed that others would complain they were chilly when the wind kicked up, while I was totally comfortable). I'm excited that my ground water temp is finally dropping again so I can do my cold showers and baths without the hassle of ice.

# Thread: Latest cholesterol results
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/latest-cholesterol-results.3908/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 16
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### jonnyh — 2012-09-25T21:10:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/latest-cholesterol-results.3908/post-54210>

So I just had my blood tested, did the usual Full blood count, iron, PSA, thyroid function, all come back in range, CRP was 1, waiting to hear the vit D result.

Fasting blood glucose was 5.2(93.6).

My cholesterol is worrying though, the LDL is still high, though since my last test in feb 12 the HDL has gone higher, and trigs have dropped, so not all bad.

Here are my results, with Feb 12 results in brackets:

Total 372(370)

LDL 266(272)

HDL 92(82)

TRIGS 65(77)

Trigs/HDL Ratio 0.70(0.93)

Total/HDL Ratio 4.04(4.48)

LDL/HDL Ratio 2.8(3.31)

I guess on the whole the trend is good, and going in the right direction, but I'm still thinking the LDL is very high.

I eat a paleish style diet, eating more fish lately, here's a rough outline of what I eat:

Salmon, , sardines, mackeral, cod, haddock 4-5 times a week,

beef and lamb once a week,

Lambs Liver once a week,

small sweet potato a day,

1 apple a day,

lots of veg(mainly kale, cabbage, spinach, carrotts),

couple of squares of dark chocolate(85%) a day,

3-4 avocadoes a week,

raw milk kefir everyday(250ml),

2-3 eggs 5 days a week,

2 TBSP of greek yoghurt a day

small amount of cheese(matchbox size) most days

EVOO on most meals

2cans coconut milk a week.

small amount of Kerrygold butter a day

Handful macadamia nuts, 1 brazil nut, small amout pumpkin seeds a day.

Organic double cream some days as a dessert.

Small glass of red wine with evening meal.

1 black coffee a day, 3-4 cups green tea, rest water.

Cinnamon, turmeric on most meals

small amount of maca powder on veggies on evening meal

Supplements:

500mg krill oil, 1g Vit C, 5000iu Vit D, 500mg Mg Citrate daily

Any advice would be appreciated regarding my high LDL, though it has come down a tiny amount, is there anything in my diet that stands out to be 'not good'?

Also anyone any tips to lower LDL?

BTW I lift weights once or twice a week, cycle twice a week, fair amount of walking, so fairly active.

I'm 5'9", 141 lbs right now.

Many thanks

# Thread: what are the signs of hypothermia?  and neoglucogenesis
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-are-the-signs-of-hypothermia-and-neoglucogenesis.3914/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 19
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### x_danny_x — 2012-09-26T01:17:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-are-the-signs-of-hypothermia-and-neoglucogenesis.3914/post-54285>

this is going to be the first cold thermogenesis through the winter for me. i am all alone.

so i am worried about hypothermia being an issue. jack mentioned it is a win win situation when dealing with cold air and cold baths. makes me feel more comfortable knowing that, but just in case i go overboard without realizing it or body starts feeling to cold, do you know anything about hypothermia creeping up on you.

also neoglucogenesis is something else that i am worried about. with almost no carbs or no carbs at all during the winter, i wonder if this pops up its nasty head on me. the body using protein as a fuel source.

### indigogirl — 2012-09-26T01:24:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-are-the-signs-of-hypothermia-and-neoglucogenesis.3914/post-54289>

IDK all the symptoms of hypothermia, but I think getting drowsy and disoriented are symptoms and that's why it's so dangerous.

Google it, I'm sure sure you'll come up with more!

### KA24 — 2012-09-26T01:27:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-are-the-signs-of-hypothermia-and-neoglucogenesis.3914/post-54290>

Gluconeogenesis is not a BAD thing. It's actually a very good adaptation that the body would die without. However, you have to match your fuel intake with what you expend. Especially if you are doing consistent CT. Without carbs, this means increases in fat. There are many recommendations out there you can find, but it's crucial that you keep your fat intake on the higher side. This won't stop Gluconeogenesis, but it will keep your body from really relying on it. You're correct, you don't want to really be in prolonged states of requiring protein conversion for energy.

### indigogirl — 2012-09-26T01:37:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-are-the-signs-of-hypothermia-and-neoglucogenesis.3914/post-54293>

Doesn't Jack say to have a meal high in fat before you CT?

### x_danny_x — 2012-09-26T02:01:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-are-the-signs-of-hypothermia-and-neoglucogenesis.3914/post-54298>

problem with googling is that the info might be wrong or outdated. shivering is one thing it says, i should say strong shivering.

this isnt just about CT, it is dealing with the cold in general. since i wont be using any heating. if things get too bad, i will just start to cover up and get warmer.

what is the limit of cold air temp that you can withstand after being cold adaptive?

jack says you can walk outside with just a regular shirt or t-shirt during the winter in an interview with Sean Croxton/Underground Wellness. it wasnt mentioned how cold is too cold to walk outside with summer clothes.

### AKMan — 2012-09-26T02:05:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-are-the-signs-of-hypothermia-and-neoglucogenesis.3914/post-54301>

I started out CTing alone as well. Set a time limit and don't exceed it the first few times, like 10 minutes. Take note of how you feel while in cold water and afterwards. Do some thinking games and warm up if you feel you are getting goofy. Two strange, advanced symptoms of hypothermia are 'paradoxial undressing' where the victim sheds all their clothing which worsens the problem, and 'terminal burrowing' also known as the 'hide-and-die' syndrome. ( [http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hypothermia](http://http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hypothermia))

It may be worth it for you to get a digital body-temp thermometer, I have one that you stick in your ear, and chart you temp for a week before undertaking CT. Then compare your temp during and after CT. If you are getting into the 95-96 range, you are in danger of hypothermia. Most see an increase in body temp after a few sessions. If you don't, you may need some advice.

Good luck and report back!

### AKMan — 2012-09-26T02:11:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-are-the-signs-of-hypothermia-and-neoglucogenesis.3914/post-54305>

> **x\_danny\_x;55026 wrote:** what is the limit of cold air temp that you can withstand after being cold adaptive?
>
>
>
> jack says you can walk outside with just a regular shirt or t-shirt during the winter in an interview with Sean Croxton/Underground Wellness. it wasnt mentioned how cold is too cold to walk outside with summer clothes.

You'll know, and it will be different for everyone. I tell people to just dress warm enough to prevent frostbite. Last year I was able to sit in an ice-filled tub of 33 degree water for an hour. Could have been longer, but gotta have a life, too. I would work outside, shovelling snow or running snow blower wearing only shorts, gloves, boots, hat in temps around -10F for at least an hour, then I'd put on a light jacket and stay out several more hours. Neighbors still think I'm nuts!

Take it slow and play it smart. A lot of this is in the mind.

### x_danny_x — 2012-09-26T02:42:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-are-the-signs-of-hypothermia-and-neoglucogenesis.3914/post-54319>

so the body has to be a constant 98 degree no matter how cold? i was thinking of buying one of the skin thermometer.

i cannot eat coconut oil which is solid. the fat that I have to consume are animal fat and like ghee? what about natural yogurt that is made from milk fat?

### KA24 — 2012-09-26T03:33:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-are-the-signs-of-hypothermia-and-neoglucogenesis.3914/post-54326>

The pre-CT meal, from my experience, is optional. It's one of those things that I would try CT with and without it (along with many other things I'd test out) and just roll with it.

### Eric — 2012-09-26T04:05:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-are-the-signs-of-hypothermia-and-neoglucogenesis.3914/post-54330>

Last winter I put on at least 5lbs of fat.

I like to experiment. Curiosity, basically. And all this fervor over 'intermittent fasting' got the better of me. So I messed with it, and I figured if I'm starving myself for 16hrs a day, I deserved a 'reward' for it. That meant Christmastime junk food. During the feeding window. Also, I was injured, so no exercise. And no CT.

So I put on _at least 5lbs of fat over that month. Maybe more. (I'm 38)

As spring approached, I knew I had to blast it off. I started exercising (power endurance, largely kettlebell work), but it was slow coming. So I decided to go "all out" and reintroduce CT, using the cool fat burner... but at this point, I'd had the working model in my hands (as opposed to the working prototype from the fall before, which while effective, needed improvements). I was gonna do whatever it took to friggen force the fat off LOL!

Anyhow, to "brute force" all that fat off, I used the device on 'hardcore' intensity (full shivering) for 2 full hours at least twice a week. Then I'd take it off, and lounge (ie, game on the computer) for 2-6 hours in the same room. Usually, that meant oscillating back and forth into shivering for the first hour, then sensations of overall chilled coolness for the rest of the session.

On the other days, I'd go to "high Moderate" intensity, ie, just short_ of shivering. So I did full shivering for 2 days a week, then strong goosebumps, bordering on shivering, for the other 3-4 days a week.

Anyhow, over those few months, my waistline dropped nearly 4 inches.

**I don't know if I was ever "hypothermic" or not, but I'm still here to tell you about it! ;D**

ps. if you go and do something dumb, don't blame me!!!

### AKMan — 2012-09-26T05:05:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-are-the-signs-of-hypothermia-and-neoglucogenesis.3914/post-54341>

> **x\_danny\_x;55047 wrote:** so the body has to be a constant 98 degree no matter how cold? i was thinking of buying one of the skin thermometer.
>
>
>
> i cannot eat coconut oil which is solid. the fat that I have to consume are animal fat and like ghee? what about natural yogurt that is made from milk fat?

That's why it's good to get a baseline before you start. Figure what your normal temp range is, then make sure you don't fall below it. The skin thermometer is good to judge your exposure, but not your core temp. Skin exposure is best at 55 degrees--core needs to be around 98.

### trishalinn@gmail.com — 2012-09-26T16:31:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-are-the-signs-of-hypothermia-and-neoglucogenesis.3914/post-54412>

> **AKMan;55029 wrote:** I started out CTing alone as well. Set a time limit and don't exceed it the first few times, like 10 minutes. Take note of how you feel while in cold water and afterwards. Do some thinking games and warm up if you feel you are getting goofy. Two strange, advanced symptoms of hypothermia are 'paradoxial undressing' where the victim sheds all their clothing which worsens the problem, and 'terminal burrowing' also known as the 'hide-and-die' syndrome. ( [http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hypothermia](http://http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hypothermia))
>
>
>
> It may be worth it for you to get a digital body-temp thermometer, I have one that you stick in your ear, and chart you temp for a week before undertaking CT. Then compare your temp during and after CT. If you are getting into the 95-96 range, you are in danger of hypothermia. Most see an increase in body temp after a few sessions. If you don't, you may need some advice.
>
>
>
> Good luck and report back!

95-96 rang? My normal body temp is 96.6 and very rarely fluctuates above 97.2. I have seen it go don to 96.1 while not CTing.

### ealachan — 2012-09-26T16:37:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-are-the-signs-of-hypothermia-and-neoglucogenesis.3914/post-54415>

> **Trisha;55182 wrote:** 95-96 rang? My normal body temp is 96.6 and very rarely fluctuates above 97.2. I have seen it go don to 96.1 while not CTing.

Don't temps that low generally indicate a thyroid issue?

### KA24 — 2012-09-26T16:49:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-are-the-signs-of-hypothermia-and-neoglucogenesis.3914/post-54417>

It can, but not always. First off, low body temperature is a relative term. It can differ from person to person, it's more important to know the set point temperature that your body regulates. This can shift from day to day, hour to hour. For most, this is impossible to know for sure unless you collect a lot of data and control many things. There are also other causes. Insulin resistance can be one, but again, that's a very broad diagnosis. Insulin resistance cause by Diabetes Type 2 for instance, would be negative. Insulin resistance, short-term, caused by a Ketogenic cycle in the diet or fasting can be a positive thing. There are also supplements and medication that can cause changes in body temperature. Finally, you have to account for measurement error. I've taken my temperature with different thermometers and using different placement methods and gotten a wide variety of readings. As I said before, with many readings using different methods and times, you may be able to get a better idea of what it is for you, but there is still some margin of error there.

I do think it is smart to monitor body temperature both during CT and outside of CT. I think the same thing about blood glucose. With enough data, I think one can identify to a better degree if there is another problem going on. Nothing is more important than how you feel during and after CT, and what changes you may notice as a result.

As I said before, with many readings using different methods and times, you may be able to get a better idea of what it is for you, but there is still some margin of error there.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/veggies-and-gut-troubles.3918/post-54373>

Follow up from webinar about veggies causing digestive issues after super low carb epi-paleo for a while:

I keep eating the veggies despite the digestion issues, correct? B/c I want those bugs to increase in numbers?

If we get digestion trouble like this from a new food or increase in a particular food.... does that really mean we need to in fact eat more of it so our gut flora will stay varied and in good numbers... kinda like prepared for anything coming in at all times? Could this be seen as a "test" for our own gut flora.... you should be able to eat a great variety of epi-paleo foods without this happening, and if we can, it means we have lots of different bugs in good numbers?

### AKMan — 2012-09-26T19:54:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/veggies-and-gut-troubles.3918/post-54482>

I read that there is about a 2 week lag in the new gut flora building in numbers sufficient for proper digestion. Another problem I see, is that vegetables should come with some bacteria that can take up residence in your gut, however, lots of the veggies we buy at the supermarket have been zapped with radiation and washed with chemicals to remove all bacteria that may make us sick and it keeps the veggies fresh longer. So, the newly introduced veggies may be so sterile that there is no bacteria in it.

I would like to see more discussion on this, ever notice that you can buy a head of lettuce at the store and it will keep for weeks? Pick a head from the garden and it turns brown and soft in a couple days. That's a clear sign it's been irradiated.

I buy organic and most of the veggies I eat really are from my garden right now  Some I wash a little, if the dirt is caked on and visible. The greens I never bother, the herbs even less. Used to feel bad about my lack of washing but I guess that was my primal voice silently directing me

I haven't had that happen with veggies keeping forever from the store? I hate buying from the store b/c things always look to me like they're ready to go to the chickens or compost  I'm spoiled from my garden I think.

If your head of lettuce from your garden goes bad that quickly you might be storing it incorrectly (give it an ice water dip the second you cut it, dry very well, store in airtight container/bag). My garden veggies keep the longest if stored correctly.

### Souldanzer — 2012-09-26T20:13:08+0300

I am noticing that my tummy is starting to calm down again  I'm about a week into increased veggies, primarily loads of dark leafies, a lot of mushrooms, and tons of herbs. I started this before EPCOTrx came out actually b/c of my Spectracell Micronutrient results.

### AKMan — 2012-09-26T20:20:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/veggies-and-gut-troubles.3918/post-54488>

> **Souldanzer;55256 wrote:** I haven't had that happen with veggies keeping forever from the store? I hate buying from the store b/c things always look to me like they're ready to go to the chickens or compost  I'm spoiled from my garden I think.

Maybe it's a regional thing. The vegetables that get shipped up here to Alaska are probably treated differently than what you guys buy in the states. Back 10 years ago, we couldn't get good veggies here, everything was spoiled as soon as it was on the shelf, now it looks great and lasts forever.

How many know about this?: [http://www.health-alternatives.com/food-supply-report.html](http://http://www.health-alternatives.com/food-supply-report.html)

Food irradiation is a process whereby the food is exposed to high levels of radiation in order to kill insects, bacteria and mold, and make the food last longer on the store shelves. Although the idea of radiating food sounds quite unappetizing to most people, it has been practiced in the USA since the 1960's, when the Food & Drug Administration approved the irradiation of wheat and white potatoes. During the 1980s, the FDA approved petitions for irradiation of spices and seasonings, pork, fresh fruits, and dry or dehydrated substances. Poultry received approval in 1990. The FDA approved irradiation for red meat in 1997.

However, studies have shown that irradiating microorganisms like E. coli and salmonella may give rise to even more dangerous, radiation-resistant strains of bacteria. Under laboratory conditions scientists found that one particular type of bacteria can survive a radiation dose five times what the FDA will allow for beef. In tests, scientists exposed this bacterium to enough radiation to kill a person several thousand times over; the bacteria survived. Before you get a false sense of security from the idea that food irradiation makes food much safer to eat - radiation is completely ineffective against viruses, and does absolutely nothing to clean the food of waste products and other unsanitary matter often left on beef, chicken, and lamb as the result of filthy and inhumane slaughterhouse conditions.

### cramernh — 2012-09-27T14:38:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/veggies-and-gut-troubles.3918/post-54724>

With all due respect: had you seen the woman in the grocery store I stopped in - who coughed DIRECTLY IN TO HER HAND (insert nervous twitch here) and then proceed to handle the oranges, you too, would be rushing to wash foods.

Also, youre damn right I went up to that woman and let her know how disgusting it was to see her cough in her hand and then manhandle the oranges. She thought it was perfectly fine where people dont eat the outer rinds.

I told her "ohhhhhh yes they do. Even if they didnt, the minute they cut into that orange with your filthy sick germs, YOU ARE GONNA GET OTHER PEOPLE SICK"... I was so frigging mad.

I am going to start colloidal silver for my SIBO. Buying CS is very, very outrageously expensive. Sometimes it is up to $25 for a few ounces and quality and safety wildly varies. I usually take about 1/4 cup to drink in the morning and in the evening depending on context and problems. That cost adds up fast. I make it for pennies on the quart in just a few hours.

I bought some professional equipment which paid for itself quickly. There are DIY systems out there but I am not so trusting of my build skills.

Here is what I use. I get no compensation for posting this here:

[http://www.thesilveredge.com/](http://http://www.thesilveredge.com/)

Everything you need to know about taking colloidal silver is on this site.

### Glamazon — 2012-09-26T20:50:10+0300

Not using it to lose weight. It is to fight infections and it works great. I am going to be using it on healing SIBO.

I drink anywhere from a fourth of a cup to to 1 cups a day when I use it. I take it nasally as with a neti pot to clear up sinuses and I have put it in my ears. It works for many things. The link I provided gives all the great uses for it.

Silver is a natural anti infective that is used on surgical and burn patients as well.

One always has to be aware of dosage, concentration and context of use.

My hormones are trashed. That makes my weight loss very problematic and frustrating. I have to fix my gut to get my hormones into optimal. When my hormones are more optimal then I should be seeing optimization in weight loss. It's a long process.

I expect for SIBO it will take more than a week of constant, structured use.

### Da-mo — 2015-02-06T12:20:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/colloidal-silver.3931/post-154898>

I make it too because I sometimes use large amounts (litres at a time) on horses for hoof/frog infections. I make it at 10ppm - goes a gold colour (if it subsequently goes greyish throw it out because the silver has coagulated) and have used it myself at times. When I first started taking it at 1 teaspoon/day I got a rash down the outside of my right leg and wondered if it was forcing something like candida to the surface. I've used it as a mouthwash and also sprayed it in eyes on both horses and people. It works great for eye infections. Once had a whole herd with conjunctivitis - sprayed them once in the morning and once in the afternoon and the next day it was gone.

In one of the webinars, Dr K. describes using it in a surgical sponge to heal a surgical wound from the inside out by changing and withdrawing the sponge as the wound closed.

### Da-mo — 2015-02-06T12:22:25+0200

### BTA — 2015-02-06T18:12:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/colloidal-silver.3931/post-154904>

the wrong form of colloidal silver is deadly and/or not effective. The home machines are garbage at best and deadly at worst. Vast majority of stuff sold via OTC is uselss and far too weak to have any effect.

There are at least 4 different forms on the market being called "COLLOIDAL SILVER":

1 electro-colloidal silver
2 mild silver protein
3 silver salts
4 powdered silver

You can only purchase the correct form via a medical provider. Ask for Bio-active Hydrosol. The form used is not "colloidal" or "ionic" - it's Angstrom Scale Colloid. The product I use contains (confirmed by lab testing) to have 1000 ppm. Far higher than anything sold OTC.

### seanb4 — 2015-02-07T12:03:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/colloidal-silver.3931/post-154956>

> [BTA said:](/goto/post?id=154904)
>
> the wrong form of colloidal silver is deadly and/or not effective. The home machines are garbage at best and deadly at worst. Vast majority of stuff sold via OTC is uselss and far too weak to have any effect.
>
> There are at least 4 different forms on the market being called "COLLOIDAL SILVER":
>
> 1 electro-colloidal silver
> 2 mild silver protein
> 3 silver salts
> 4 powdered silver
>
>
> You can only purchase the correct form via a medical provider. Ask for Bio-active Hydrosol. The form used is not "colloidal" or "ionic" - it's Angstrom Scale Colloid. The product I use contains (confirmed by lab testing) to have 1000 ppm. Far higher than anything sold OTC.

Can anyone confirm/refute this?

Why is bio-active hydrosol the only good one. What is different?

Like I said, I'm getting some results from the silver biotics so it has to be doing something... is that a hyrosol?

### Da-mo — 2015-02-07T12:48:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/colloidal-silver.3931/post-154957>

The generator I have works by electrolysis - a small transformer connected with two alligator clips to two silver electrodes/rods suspended in distilled water. I measure strength with a ppm meter and aim for 10ppm. Anything above about 12-13 ppm and the silver begins to plate out onto the glass container.

The company I bought the devices from also makes colloidal silver which looks and works exactly the same as the stuff I make - I've used their product as well. Their product was tested at 60% colloidal silver and 40% ionic silver.

I dunno about all the arguments about what is and isn't colloidal silver proper - I just know the stuff I make at home works.

### SeaHorse — 2015-02-07T13:31:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/colloidal-silver.3931/post-154960>

Sounds like the same machine I have…and the stuff I have made has worked for infections really well. I also have bought silver OTC and have had good results with it….

> [seanb4 said:](/goto/post?id=154896)
>
> I have been taking this for about a week at roughly 12 tbsps per day and noticed a significant decrease in heart palpitations.
> What is the action behind its effect? Is it just by the silver coming into content with bacteria and killing them? Does it do anything else?
> Have my palpitations reduced by the silver killing bacteria in my blood stream / gut allowing my immune system to deal with other stuff (leaky gut?) causing less overall stress to my body and thus less palps; Or is another mechanism at play?

If the silver is getting at infection, I would image you are going to see a lot of changes….that is it's primary effect as far a I know.

### SeaHorse — 2015-02-07T13:36:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/colloidal-silver.3931/post-154961>

> [seanb4 said:](/goto/post?id=154956)
>
> Can anyone confirm/refute this?
>
> Why is bio-active hydrosol the only good one. What is different?
>
> Like I said, I'm getting some results from the silver biotics so it has to be doing something... is that a hyrosol?

I have a veterinary silver formula that is chelated…they sell to the profession and claim that this works better for wounds than other forms. I really can't answer your question Sean, but I would go with your results and keep researching. Every silver product claims to be the only one that works….it's just the way it is. Good luck

### Da-mo — 2015-02-07T13:42:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/colloidal-silver.3931/post-154962>

> [seanb4 said:](/goto/post?id=154896)
>
> I have been taking this for about a week at roughly 12 tbsps per day and noticed a significant decrease in heart palpitations.
> What is the action behind its effect? Is it just by the silver coming into content with bacteria and killing them? Does it do anything else?
> Have my palpitations reduced by the silver killing bacteria in my blood stream / gut allowing my immune system to deal with other stuff (leaky gut?) causing less overall stress to my body and thus less palps; Or is another mechanism at play?

Silver, as a colloid, is reported to kill over 600 disease causing organisms like, bacteria, viruses, fungi and parasites. This happens in a matter of minutes upon direct contact. The silver does not attack them directly, but destroys the enzymes they depend on for oxygen. Therefore resistant strains should not be able to develop and the body does not develop a tolerance. [http://www.healthhouse.co.nz/pamphlets/colloidal-silver-generator-instructions-(738kb).pdf](http://www.healthhouse.co.nz/pamphlets/colloidal-silver-generator-instructions-\(738kb\).pdf)

# Thread: Having technical support issues on the new site?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/having-technical-support-issues-on-the-new-site.3933/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### tellmisty — 2012-09-26T20:51:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/having-technical-support-issues-on-the-new-site.3933/post-54503>

Hi all,

Okay, we have created a page where we're starting to compile FAQs to help you guys with common questions. [You can view it here](http://http://jackkruse.com/member-dashboard/support-faqs/).

If you're stuck and need help or have a frustration, complaint, or concern, please submit a ticket. The forums is not the place to get help. We are going to start deleting posts where people are complaining or trying to get support assistance. It's very challenging to for the technical support team to have 40 places to check to make sure people are taken care of. If you have an issue, [submit a ticket](http://https://jackkruse.zendesk.com/home).

Thanks!

# Thread: Why do I suddenly have much dryer skin?

**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread

══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### bigknitwit — 2012-09-26T21:26:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-do-i-suddenly-have-much-dryer-skin.3937/post-54525>

On the heals of Jack sharing his labs, I am noticing seasonal differences setting in.

The skin on my hands, which had been fantastic is now drying out and getting rough. My kids lips are starting to chap, and they have all caught some cold (not me yet). What accounts for this?

### Huck — 2012-09-26T22:30:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-do-i-suddenly-have-much-dryer-skin.3937/post-54553>

Vitamin D, is my guess. Vitamin D gives you moist skin. Lack of vitamin d gives you dry, flaky skin.

### Stavity — 2012-09-27T01:22:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-do-i-suddenly-have-much-dryer-skin.3937/post-54625>

My skin was dry eating paleo, but now that I added, mainly, fish to my diet it is becoming smooth and soft again. Hands is where i noticed it the most. Still have a ways to go with my skin, but feeling very hopeful.

### Doug — 2012-09-27T02:48:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-do-i-suddenly-have-much-dryer-skin.3937/post-54643>

I have also noticed dry skin, especially on back of hand, ... Turgor test is bad allof a sudden.

Getting lab results next week.... Hopefully that will help explain reason.

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-09-27T03:19:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-do-i-suddenly-have-much-dryer-skin.3937/post-54651>

add me to the club - feet are so dry and i've even started itching all over. I eat fish twice a day but don't supplement fish oil any more. Supp D...IDK

### trishalinn@gmail.com — 2012-09-27T05:52:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-do-i-suddenly-have-much-dryer-skin.3937/post-54687>

### Inger — 2012-09-27T08:44:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-do-i-suddenly-have-much-dryer-skin.3937/post-54705>

I am thinking it is detox of a sort. I had terribly dry skin first months.. now last weeks it have gotten so smooth! Like velvet. I wonder if it has something to do with my seaweed consumption, I drink seaweed water every day and eat seaweed too. Fish, 2-3 times / day. I guess, when you do this too, everything will be just nice soon enough. You need to have some patience, there is much stuff to detox for sure... I am giving myself much time.. I just go on doing what I know what is right and do not care if results do not come immidiately. After all.. I hurted my body for 35 years. huh.

I do CT but not much, just a short swim in the river every day, about 200 m at +9 degree C. I cannot deal with too much detox right now because of the stress of my divorce, losing my home and all. I have no money to buy milkthistle or other supps eather so I do it my way, baby steps.  I avoid fake light as much as I can too. I have to say it works so well and I enjoy the trip so very much!

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-do-i-suddenly-have-much-dryer-skin.3937/post-55721>

> **Anna K.;56869 wrote:** My skin is also a bit dry, so I wonder if it's because of CT?

Several things can give you dry skin

1) like Huck said, it could be you are low on Vitamin D

2) low vitamin B12

3) omega 6/3 ratio is way above 4/1.

### cuivie@me.com — 2012-10-02T06:56:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-do-i-suddenly-have-much-dryer-skin.3937/post-55768>

On a related note, when I added seaweed into my previpusly all-carnivore (paleo) diet, I got dandruff. The only other time in my life when I've had dandruff is a cpuple of months ago when I briefly added brazil nuts into an otherwise all-carnivore diet. When I stopped the nuts, the dandruff went away. I've dropped the seaweed, waiting to see if the dandruff will go away now. For now eating raw carnivore Epi-Paleo (5 oysters every day, regular grassfed beef liver, regular mackerel and salmon, grassfed chuck).

### bigknitwit — 2012-10-02T17:34:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-do-i-suddenly-have-much-dryer-skin.3937/post-55827>

My skin has been wonderfull all summer (except for the elusive rough heels, but they've been that way forever), and it's only as the season has changed that I've noticed the skin change. Diet has been basically the same (seafood, etc), except I started supplementing D. If it were an Omega issue, why does the summer mask it skin-wise?

### nonchalant — 2012-10-02T18:17:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-do-i-suddenly-have-much-dryer-skin.3937/post-55842>

CT 6 talks about the body moving Omega 6 fats around in the body when it starts to get cold. Perhaps it is stealing from the skin to go to more important areas?

### bigknitwit — 2012-10-02T21:06:05+0300

### Stavity — 2012-10-05T01:05:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-do-i-suddenly-have-much-dryer-skin.3937/post-56351>

> **bigknitwit;57115 wrote:** Doesn't it take upwards of 2 years to correct an O3:O6 ratio problem? Maybe I'm just not there yet and need to keep up with the fish...

I believe it does take time, but I am finally seeing a little results. Guess, whatever, I was doing before epi-paleo was not good. This is truly a diet that works for me and not against.

### hope2conceive — 2012-10-06T18:27:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-do-i-suddenly-have-much-dryer-skin.3937/post-56602>

> **CuiviÃ«;56930 wrote:** On a related note, when I added seaweed into my previpusly all-carnivore (paleo) diet, I got dandruff. The only other time in my life when I've had dandruff is a cpuple of months ago when I briefly added brazil nuts into an otherwise all-carnivore diet. When I stopped the nuts, the dandruff went away. I've dropped the seaweed, waiting to see if the dandruff will go away now. For now eating raw carnivore Epi-Paleo (5 oysters every day, regular grassfed beef liver, regular mackerel and salmon, grassfed chuck).

Hi Cuivie,

(hope no one minds my asking this right here)....how do you eat your oysters - plain? And how about the liver? I would love to know....

### cuivie@me.com — 2012-10-07T06:10:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-do-i-suddenly-have-much-dryer-skin.3937/post-56673>

> **hope2conceive;57889 wrote:** Hi Cuivie,
>
>
>
> (hope no one minds my asking this right here)....how do you eat your oysters - plain? And how about the liver? I would love to know....

I keep things simple.......plain and raw for both. I hate complex food preparation, and to me it should never take more time to prepare food than it does to eat it. It's a shame to eat oysters anything other than raw, and I dislike the texture of cooked liver (or even cooked meat...rare only for me, please). Sometimes I use seal salt on my liver and beef, but frequently just plain and raw. Warming up to body temp (of the animal) is all the cooking/preparing that I do other than slicing into desired portions and putting on a plate. Occasionally I do grill, searing with high heat (500-600F) while the inside stays raw/blue.

Recently I tried adding in some EPCOTx spices onto the food directly, but spices screw up my satiety control (a la Stephan Guyenet's work on food reward), so I've stopped that and get the EPCOTx spices all in one shot in one half-yummy/half-gross cocktail. Food is back to raw and plain (sometimes salted).

I ditched all vegetables over a year and a half ago and haven't looked back since, except for the aforementioned small seaweed excursion. It's back to pure raw carnivory for me. Just how my body works best. YMMV.

### TheKid — 2012-10-07T17:10:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-do-i-suddenly-have-much-dryer-skin.3937/post-56717>

I would imagine T3 could be an issue as well. If thryoid function isn't optimal, skin and hair problems are common. And if you are low carb but have yet to optimize your iodine, then your T3 may not be great. The minute I went low carb, I had more problems with skin. It makes sense since I didn't know I already had Hashis. And I hadn't yet gone Epi-Paleo. So Free T3 might be something to tetst.

### hope2conceive — 2012-10-09T02:38:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-do-i-suddenly-have-much-dryer-skin.3937/post-57000>

> **CuiviÃ«;57973 wrote:** I keep things simple.......plain and raw for both. I hate complex food preparation, and to me it should never take more time to prepare food than it does to eat it. It's a shame to eat oysters anything other than raw, and I dislike the texture of cooked liver (or even cooked meat...rare only for me, please). Sometimes I use seal salt on my liver and beef, but frequently just plain and raw. Warming up to body temp (of the animal) is all the cooking/preparing that I do other than slicing into desired portions and putting on a plate. Occasionally I do grill, searing with high heat (500-600F) while the inside stays raw/blue.
>
>
>
> Recently I tried adding in some EPCOTx spices onto the food directly, but spices screw up my satiety control (a la Stephan Guyenet's work on food reward), so I've stopped that and get the EPCOTx spices all in one shot in one half-yummy/half-gross cocktail. Food is back to raw and plain (sometimes salted).
>
>
>
> I ditched all vegetables over a year and a half ago and haven't looked back since, except for the aforementioned small seaweed excursion. It's back to pure raw carnivory for me. Just how my body works best. YMMV.

Whoa, that is amazing. Not sure I could do all that raw. Used to do small amounts of liver raw in a smoothie. So you don't miss veggies at all?

Also, what is YMMV?

Thanks for the inspiration. I just got my first shipment of cod liver today...hope I can stomach it.

### cuivie@me.com — 2012-10-09T07:38:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-do-i-suddenly-have-much-dryer-skin.3937/post-57063>

> **hope2conceive;58302 wrote:** Whoa, that is amazing. Not sure I could do all that raw. Used to do small amounts of liver raw in a smoothie.
> My roommate has tried raw liver and can barely stand it. I got into one day when I was preparing turkey gizzards and liver. All of a sudden the raw liver just appealed to me strongly...the texture, the smell....suddenly got the urge to taste it.....popped it into my mouth and it was the most creamiest, delicious thing ever. Can't stand cooked liver ever since.
>
>
>
> Now, that desire for liver (raw, of course) comes in phases. After I'd had my fill I don't crave it or even like it much for a while. Then, later, the liver frenzy besets me again until I've satisfied the hunger for it and the desire wanes again. Rinse, repeat.
>
>
>
>
> > So you don't miss veggies at all?
> > Nope. Not at all. They are nice every once in a while...broccoli, asparagus, mushrooms, onions, fresh garlic....during the summer. But I like grassfed chuck steak a WHOLE friggin' lot more. I'd rather spend my hard-earned money on the fatty, gamey, meat. I needs it. I craves it. Forget the damn veggies.
> >
> >
> >
> >
> >
> > Also, what is YMMV?
>
> Your Mileage May Vary. Some people do great on a strict carnivore diet, others find they need some veggies (so-called "dirty carnivore" style diet). Although I wonder if those people were to do Epi-Paleo carnivore with plenty of seafood....
>
>
>
>
>
> Thanks for the inspiration. I just got my first shipment of cod liver today...hope I can stomach it.

Awesome! I do find fish liver to be a bit strong in taste...like it stings your tongue. Or I guess that was the fish's liver that did that. I'm admittedly not so up on my fish anatomy...I just grabbed some guts and chucked the whole glob down my pie hole. Coulda been some other organ that was so bitter.

Let us know how it goes!

### SueC — 2012-09-26T21:39:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/testing-for-hashis.3938/post-54531>

Time to talk with my doc. Is the TPO antibodies the best test to get for Hashi's?

### Patty Cakes — 2012-09-26T21:40:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/testing-for-hashis.3938/post-54533>

You need both the TPO and TgAB for Hashis. You could have positive antibodies in one and not the other. Basically you need both tests to rule it out.

# Thread: 7-Keto: Brand matters
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/7-keto-brand-matters.3941/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 39
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### SueC — 2012-09-26T21:51:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/7-keto-brand-matters.3941/post-54539>

Pharmacist just called with some lab results. Testosterone so high it couldn't be measured! I had no overt symptoms, but I was about half-way through a one-month bottle of 100 mg 7-Keto DHEA. Lab said this result is becoming more common. A lot of 7-keto on the market really isn't or is adulterated with at least some DHEA. (And Dr. Oz recommends 100 mg twice daily for weight loss!)

Brand I used was Source Naturals. SO, it pays to buy better brands of supplements AND as the doc says, you have to test!

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-09-26T21:58:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/7-keto-brand-matters.3941/post-54542>

Interesting!!!!! I am doing 7-keto, too and my T is now up to 6X top of range. I thought I bought some good stuff tho. I

bought the NutraBio, supposed to be the best one out there.

### SueC — 2012-10-02T23:33:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/7-keto-brand-matters.3941/post-55956>

I FINALLY heard back from Source Naturals with this answer:

"The answers to your questions are as follows: we purchase this branded supplement 7-keto DHEA from the patent holder Humanetics Corp. which is located in Minnesota. According to the Certificates of Analysis (C of A) they provide with the material it is manufactured in China.

The manufacture on their C of A provide test results for ID, purity, lead, heavy metals, yeast, molds, & pathogens.

As a responsible manufacture of supplements once Threshold receives raw materials we again test for all of these items. We test each lot we receive to confirm ID, purity, lead, heavy metals, yeast, molds, & pathogens."

In talking with a company rep "off the record", he believes the research for stating that 7-keto doesn't convert is poor. He could find only 1 study using a handful of "healthy men." I can't find any more than that study, either, but I'll keep trying.

I talked with the folks at Life Extension about conversion and that person said 7-keto can raise testosterone scores slightly, but nothing like my off the charts reading.

So we're still left with buyer be wary, and TEST, TEST, TEST!

I'll give the basics as it relates to this thread.

3 testosterone tests, all same lab, range for all three was 7-46.

1\. 8/23, had been taking DHEA 50 mgs, and 7-KETO 100mgs for about two weeks.

Total Testosterone 152. Stopped regular DHEA that day.

2\. 9/7, still taking 7-KETO 100mgs for a little over a month at this point.

Total Testosterone 315. Stopped 7Keto that day.

3\. 11/1, DHEA supps free.

Total Testosterone 28

I also re-checked Vit-D after a bio-hack. That went from

8/23, 21 Vit D, 25-OH, Total.

11/1, 77 Vit D, 25-OH, Total.

### SueC — 2013-08-20T20:12:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/7-keto-brand-matters.3941/post-93255>

Finally found something that might explain my super high T results while taking 7-keto DHEA. This lab recommends stopping the supplement 3-5 days before testing because some labs are using antibodies that cross-react with 7-keto DHEA. So maybe it's not "brand matters" but lab matters....

[http://www.zrtlab.com/professionals...the-best-results-when-monitoring-dhea-therapy](http://www.zrtlab.com/professionals/zrt-blogs/entry/how-to-get-the-best-results-when-monitoring-dhea-therapy)

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/odd-senations-in-my-ears-and-diaphragm.3956/post-54708>

well, Ive just got to the eight week period of the Leptin reset. Its been a journey of odd things, The latest seems to be a pulsing pressure feeling in my ears with,particularly at night a sound like a distant fog horn ! low and pulsing. I also have this pressure in the diaphragm and still slightly exagerated heart beat despite thinking that the potassium /magnesium supplementing had sorted things out. In fact I feel low level crappiness.

Is this just another passing phase or do I need to address it? If so, how ?

Also I have lost no weight ,in fact I am 2 lbs heavier.I expected that as JK said it would happen ,it did. On the + side my sleep is so much better, I am not so crabby and I have better energy. My skin looks better too. I am thinking that I will just continue and see how things progress but I wish I felt better.

I have recently started brewing and drinking kombucha, that really has settled the constipation problem.

thanks to all who respond

### nonchalant — 2012-09-28T01:02:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/odd-senations-in-my-ears-and-diaphragm.3956/post-54882>

Hi Somewhat, have you had a test for inflammation? HS-CRP?

### somewhatchubby — 2012-09-28T09:31:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/odd-senations-in-my-ears-and-diaphragm.3956/post-54947>

Hi Nonchalant , no I havnt. I'm off to the docs today, as combined with hot flushes from being post menopause ,its not alot of fun.

I'll look into thy HS-CRP.

thanks.

### somewhatchubby — 2012-10-12T23:48:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/odd-senations-in-my-ears-and-diaphragm.3956/post-57878>

thanks to those who replied. I have been having repeat performances on and off since I first posted about it. Last night I slept little ,I kept having to get up from bed, my heart was racing,pounding under my ribs and into my throat and I had this lump of concrete under my ribs. I thought I was having a heart attack except I had an ECG last week and nothing bad came up . Ive been following various threads in connection with the gut and candi da and whilst searcing today came up with this. This lady excatly described my symptoms from last night/ Dhe has Heliobacter Pylori. She has also been unable to lose wieight....DING !

[http://amyking.wordpress.com/2009/02/20/im-so-happy-i-have-h-pylori/](http://http://amyking.wordpress.com/2009/02/20/im-so-happy-i-have-h-pylori/)

I;m going to take another read and implement some of the suggestions.

One question came to mind. I am getting some milk Kefir evvery day . If I use Manuka honey will it negate the benefits of the bacteria in the Kefir? also is CO effective against H. Pylori ?

thanks.

# Thread: Article on Fox news about light from e-readers and sleep
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/article-on-fox-news-about-light-from-e-readers-and-sleep.3960/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### melrito — 2012-09-27T15:32:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/article-on-fox-news-about-light-from-e-readers-and-sleep.3960/post-54733>

thought everyone might like seeing this...

[http://www.foxnews.com/tech/2012/09/26/dont-let-your-e-reader-keep-up-at-night/?cmpid=cmty\_other\_Don%27t\_let\_your\_e-Reader\_keep\_you\_up\_at\_night](http://http://www.foxnews.com/tech/2012/09/26/dont-let-your-e-reader-keep-up-at-night/?cmpid=cmty_other_Don%27t_let_your_e-Reader_keep_you_up_at_night)

### Optimalbound — 2012-09-27T21:10:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/article-on-fox-news-about-light-from-e-readers-and-sleep.3960/post-54807>

Thanks for posting. There are some inaccuracies in the article, as I see it.

> Kindles and Nooks that lack a built-in light cannot adjust the screen, but because the device's e-ink displays have lower contrast, these e-readers are less disruptive to sleep.

In this context, the essential thing about the e-ink display is not the contrast, but the fact that it does not emit any light of its own. So one is free to use any light. Whenever I use my Kindle Keyboard in bed, I use a monochrome red LED lamp as my sole light source.

### melrito — 2012-09-27T23:04:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/article-on-fox-news-about-light-from-e-readers-and-sleep.3960/post-54853>

I totally agree the article doesn't cover everything - no mention of TVs or computers at night either, but it does show that some awareness is being made that artificial light does disrupt sleep (there is of course no mention of everything else it disrupts over time).

# Thread: funny moments in Epi-Paleo living
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/funny-moments-in-epi-paleo-living.3965/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 21
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ealachan — 2012-09-27T19:20:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/funny-moments-in-epi-paleo-living.3965/post-54767>

We need more levity around here. I want to hear about peoples' funny Epi-Paleo moments. I'll go first - naturally, it involves the bathroom...

I was in the bathroom a few minutes ago taking a pee, and the smell of garlic engulfed me! Not just like a vague smell of garlic, either...but a very specific and strong toasted garlic smell. I sat there sniffing and thinking "my pee smells like garlic?! What does THAT mean?! OMG, I gotta go Ask Jack!" As I finished up in the bathroom and washed my hands, the smell kept getting stronger and stronger. I had flushed by this point, so it COULDN'T be my pee. Then I realized: there are bagels downstairs in the kitchen at work, and the kitchen is directly under the bathroom next to my office. Someone was toasting a garlic bagel for lunch, and it had me completely convinced for a minute or two that I'd had some kind of spectacular systemic breakthrough (or breakdown?!).

Well, it made ME laugh, anyway. Who's next?

### Optimalbound — 2012-09-27T20:53:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/funny-moments-in-epi-paleo-living.3965/post-54795>

I don't have a very funny story, but here's a little CT episode:

This Easter, I shared a hotel room for a week with a good friend of mine (who doesn't know about my CTing). One evening, I was in in the 50 degrees bath tub and reading a book when he came back to our room after some shopping.

Me: "I'm in the bathroom, having a soak!"

Him: "What?? Are you having a bath _again_?! You're not using up all the hot water, are you?"

Me: "Nope... Don't worry...."

### Souldanzer — 2012-09-27T22:53:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/funny-moments-in-epi-paleo-living.3965/post-54847>

The roommate getting into your left-over food in the fridge while you are asleep. He then shows up the next morning a little green in the face.......... what can I say, most of my food is LOADED optimally...... half lb turmeric, a whole thing of garlic, almost raw onions, ..... things that make you DETOX and HEAL and fix your GUT...... lol. I think he cleared out some biofilm that night

My food is safe now.

### ealachan — 2012-09-27T23:22:51+0300

Haha!

I said "canned salmon, you want some?" and held the can out...before I'd mashed any of the skin and bones at all. He practically ran away, saying "NO THANK YOU!". It was hilarious.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/funny-moments-in-epi-paleo-living.3965/post-54868>

# Thread: Food as medicine and not entertainment
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/food-as-medicine-and-not-entertainment.3967/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### indigogirl — 2012-09-27T19:40:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/food-as-medicine-and-not-entertainment.3967/post-54771>

I am being more conscious of my food choices and noticing that in the past (recent past!) many of my choices were dictated by the entertainment value of food, not what would fuel my Ferrari.

So I am making myself eat seafood at least once a day, preferably 2x. MHS are out of the question so far, as is any raw seafood. I will build up to raw oysters! I'm eating fermented food with at least 2 meals. Need to eat more seaweed...so far toasted nori.

Curious to know how epi-Paleo is affecting your food choices?

### ealachan — 2012-09-27T19:53:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/food-as-medicine-and-not-entertainment.3967/post-54773>

I think I've definitely had this change lately as well. I'm finding it a lot easier to tell myself "yes, that beer would taste great, but it's just as bad as eating bread, so let's not", or "kelp really isn't the tastiest thing in the world to me, but eating it will do me good, so down the hatch it goes".

### prAna303 — 2012-09-27T20:58:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/food-as-medicine-and-not-entertainment.3967/post-54800>

Since i cleaned up my food choices for about two years ago and started to eat only what would be available in my region (seafoods) i completely lost emotions about eating. I see food as an energychoice to experience other feelings. I really do feel new thing nowdays, like music i can sense subtile details (classical and other instrumental). The light in the woods makes me go crazy, in a good way. The smells on top of a mountain, wow! ItÂ´s amazing when the addiction to feeling concerning food starts to loose itÂ´s grip on your psyche.

### AKMan — 2012-09-27T22:31:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/food-as-medicine-and-not-entertainment.3967/post-54839>

I have been playing around with the FitDay.com nutrient tool and came up with this awesome menu. Could be split up over a day or eaten all at once. Hits a minimum of 100% on every tracked nutrient. If you take anything away you fall below 100% somewhere.

Can anyone else beat this?

It's only 1400 calories:

8 oz (2 cans) Smoked Oysters

8 oz Chicken livers cooked in 1TBS Butter

1/2 yellow bell pepper

2oz Almonds

3oz Swiss Cheese

6 strips dried seaweed

1397 Calories, 87g Fat, 38g tot carbs, 116g Protein

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/55666=482-nut.JPG)

The light in the woods makes me go crazy, in a good way. The smells on top of a mountain, wow! ItÂ´s amazing when the addiction to feeling concerning food starts to loose itÂ´s grip on your psyche.

You're senses have become much more acute!!

Looking forward to the day when food loses its grip on my psyche.

### nonchalant — 2012-09-27T22:47:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/food-as-medicine-and-not-entertainment.3967/post-54845>

> **prAna303;55623 wrote:** Since i cleaned up my food choices for about two years ago and started to eat only what would be available in my region (seafoods) i completely lost emotions about eating. I see food as an energychoice to experience other feelings. I really do feel new thing nowdays, like music i can sense subtile details (classical and other instrumental). The light in the woods makes me go crazy, in a good way. The smells on top of a mountain, wow! ItÂ´s amazing when the addiction to feeling concerning food starts to loose itÂ´s grip on your psyche.

Beautifully said.

When you're optimal, you lose the fear of being hungry, and the false bliss of the plant opioids and cannabinoids.

Now you can quit being obsessed about food, and go live your life.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-09-27T23:21:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/food-as-medicine-and-not-entertainment.3967/post-54858>

I am a recovering foodie. I am a recovering baker. I used to love the nuances in food. If it was unique, I would try it. New restaurants in town, I'd be there within two weeks of opening. Now, although I am still fascinated by food, it sits in a different place in my heart. I no longer have a love affair w/ food, I eat for brain nutrition and brain fuel. I still try to prepare it in a fun way but usually that only involves spices. But I learned 15 years ago about using spices and fresh herbs to add to the food and what a difference it can make. I have eaten organic most of my life and have always been fairly thin (except for early thyroid days) but I adored food. No more love affair and I am not sad that we broke up.

I can't wait for my senses to heighten!

### SeaHorse — 2012-09-28T00:44:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/food-as-medicine-and-not-entertainment.3967/post-54876>

I agree...the love affair with food is often a sadly abusive addiction...sometimes I still can miss a certain texture or feel, but freedom is intoxicating. It's true too that our other senses are often ignored when we are food junkies. I've been walking in our woods and it's almost peak leaf colour here in Vermont and I'm drinking it in every day. It is almost like being in an altered state of mind, but it's congruent and there is no damage or hangover...nature's last big party before the cold, sleepy winter.

### janagram — 2012-10-03T01:54:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/food-as-medicine-and-not-entertainment.3967/post-55977>

wow! Akman, this is great! But, holy moly, what about berries? what about eggs? spinach? And please, can't I have wild salmon instead of oysters? (I hear a "Noooooooo"!)

what about ....Baaaaaconnnn!!???

# Thread: Comfort food cravings - no desire for seafood!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/comfort-food-cravings-no-desire-for-seafood.3969/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BJK77 — 2012-09-27T20:03:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/comfort-food-cravings-no-desire-for-seafood.3969/post-54777>

I've had a cold for a few days now. Every time I think it's starting to get better I end up feeling even worse. The cold itself isn't all that terrible just a slight runny nose, but I am absolutely exhausted. I feel so drained it's tough to make it through a day.

The worst part is I have zero desire for seafood right now. I'm still forcing it in, but I'm craving my childhood comfort foods like MAD. For me, this is things like my mom's homemade chicken pot pie, pasta alfredo, chicken corn soup, chocolate chip cookies . . . you get the gist - gluten-filled, non AI-protocol friendly JUNK.

I've been on some sort of a grain-free/GAPS/Paleo diet for 3 years now and honestly haven't really had many cravings. Once I cut out grains my cravings dropped right off, but this is almost unbearable!

My teeth are also killing me which makes me want to bite down on something super crunchy . . . maybe a bowl of Cap'n Crunch. UGH! I know I'd never give in and eat SAD crap again, but wow is it tempting right now.

Bizarre! Anyone else have something similar happen when you were sick? I need to fight this off.

### cramernh — 2012-09-27T20:05:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/comfort-food-cravings-no-desire-for-seafood.3969/post-54778>

Honestly, when Im sick as a dog, all I want is homemade chicken soup. I just cooked a 5 gallon batch for my husband who is fighting a very nasty viral respiratory infection. Everything right down to the stock is freshly made.

I would highly suggest making iced cold water as your friend to help 'fill up'... its rare that I have sweet-urges thanks to being a low carber now for almost 18 months. When it does come around, I fill up my 64oz jug with ice water and Im good to go.

### indigogirl — 2012-09-27T20:19:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/comfort-food-cravings-no-desire-for-seafood.3969/post-54780>

I, too, crave comfort foods when I'm sick, especially when it's a stomach virus. Then all I want is broth with noodles.

I'm sure you'll be able to fight it off! Fill up on broths and warm teas??

### vlynnb — 2012-09-27T21:16:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/comfort-food-cravings-no-desire-for-seafood.3969/post-54812>

I just had a cold and I really didn't want fish either, just comfort food. And cold water just gives me the chills. Daughter made a big pot of chicken soup for her family. I settled for eaitng all the things that would be in my chicken soup minus the chicken. The raw crushed garlic clove I ate though about killed me, burned all the way down. Garlic capsules combined with hot pepper and zicam took care of the cold in a few days rather than the 10 it used to take me to get over something and then fish came back into the menu. Don't understand the fish aversion thing either.

### Souldanzer — 2012-09-27T22:01:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/comfort-food-cravings-no-desire-for-seafood.3969/post-54834>

> **BJK77;55600 wrote:** I've had a cold for a few days now. Every time I think it's starting to get better I end up feeling even worse. The cold itself isn't all that terrible just a slight runny nose, but I am absolutely exhausted. I feel so drained it's tough to make it through a day.
>
>
>
> The worst part is I have zero desire for seafood right now. I'm still forcing it in, but I'm craving my childhood comfort foods like MAD. For me, this is things like my mom's homemade chicken pot pie, pasta alfredo, chicken corn soup, chocolate chip cookies . . . you get the gist - gluten-filled, non AI-protocol friendly JUNK.
>
>
>
> I've been on some sort of a grain-free/GAPS/Paleo diet for 3 years now and honestly haven't really had many cravings. Once I cut out grains my cravings dropped right off, but this is almost unbearable!
>
>
>
> My teeth are also killing me which makes me want to bite down on something super crunchy . . . maybe a bowl of Cap'n Crunch. UGH! I know I'd never give in and eat SAD crap again, but wow is it tempting right now.
>
>
>
> Bizarre! Anyone else have something similar happen when you were sick? I need to fight this off.

read Dr K's reply to my food poisoning causing AN relapse....... if you're sick you're oxidizing as I understand. You want to reduce reduce reduce...... DHA/iodine, oxytoxin, melatonin, DHEA..... I might be off here.

I'd make some chicken soup that's paleo-friendly and see if that keeps cravings in check and do other comforting things.

### nonchalant — 2012-09-27T22:51:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/comfort-food-cravings-no-desire-for-seafood.3969/post-54846>

> **BJK77;55600 wrote:**
>
> The worst part is I have zero desire for seafood right now. I'm still forcing it in, but I'm craving my childhood comfort foods like MAD. For me, this is things like my mom's homemade chicken pot pie, pasta alfredo, chicken corn soup, chocolate chip cookies . . . you get the gist - gluten-filled, non AI-protocol friendly JUNK.

Your brain is remembering what made you feel good in the past.

Gotta love those opioids and cannabinoids. Yummy!

### BJK77 — 2012-09-28T16:38:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/comfort-food-cravings-no-desire-for-seafood.3969/post-54996>

I made it through the day without caving, but it is just amazing to me how strong these cravings are. The very odd thing is that I really haven't had any typical illnesses (cold flu, etc) in the last 3-4 years since my health started spiraling downward. It's strange that now that I am adopting such a healthy lifestyle I suddenly come down with a minor cold that has just totally landed me on my butt!

I know there are so many different theories out there about how and why we succumb to these minor illnesses, one of which I believe is that it's actually healthy to get sick on occasion. I think the theory is that it's akin to your body cleaning house and finally being able to address some of the viruses you've been exposed to. Someone explained it to me once saying that the immune system is like a housekeeper. It takes care of what it can and when it's already stressed to the max dirt/germs get stored away in corners and crevices unable to be dealt with. As the housekeeper finally starts to catch up on the "work" it can take on more and begin to tackle the tucked away dust and germs.

Anyone ever hear something similar? What do you think?

### jeepifer@gmail.com — 2012-09-28T17:52:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/comfort-food-cravings-no-desire-for-seafood.3969/post-55012>

Have you tried Yarrow tea? It's very cleansing. The key to it is, if it tastes good, you need to drink it! Drink a cup or two or three, until it tastes bitter. Then you're done. I know it sounds weird, but it really does work!

### Destiny — 2012-09-28T22:18:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/comfort-food-cravings-no-desire-for-seafood.3969/post-55074>

I am not crazy about seafood lately either but I get it down somehow. And not only that, I feel like having bread, pea soup, and a lot of potatoes! I did not miss it or think of it for almost a year. Something is changing.

Could this be due to the change in seasons?

### SeaHorse — 2012-09-28T22:26:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/comfort-food-cravings-no-desire-for-seafood.3969/post-55078>

That's what I think...the equinox was a real turning point for me...I'm feeling the cold, I don't want to CT and I'm wanting to be indoors wrapped in a blanket. I jumped in our pond yesterday and was ok while I was in...but holy moses I shivered for over an hour afterwards and had to take a warm shower...that hasn't been my CT experience since spring. I did stop swimming in early Sept. too. It's super hard to get going in the right direction and embrace the cold...particularly because I know my thyroid and hormones are still yuck.

### prAna303 — 2012-09-28T22:55:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/comfort-food-cravings-no-desire-for-seafood.3969/post-55085>

> **BJK77;55600 wrote:** I've had a cold for a few days now. Every time I think it's starting to get better I end up feeling even worse. The cold itself isn't all that terrible just a slight runny nose, but I am absolutely exhausted. I feel so drained it's tough to make it through a day.

For me, this is things like my mom's homemade chicken pot pie, pasta alfredo, chicken corn soup, chocolate chip cookies . . . you get the gist - gluten-filled, non AI-protocol friendly JUNK.
>

>
