Perhaps itÂ´s as simple as this: your craving is deep rooted in your psyche because when you where a kid your parents gave you food as an inverted reward when you where home from school. When you now optimizing your life old memories come up to the surface and when you get sick your system goes to the autopilot mode and you find comfort in your past. Think about why you have such emotional connections to food and use it to take a new step. When we get out of the comfortzone of the modern world and get scared, sick and whatever we often find a relief in doing what we used to do... Simply put, we get scared the \*\*\*\* out of our primal senses when we get in touch with them. Use it and start a new behavior!

### Momma28 — 2012-09-29T20:05:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/comfort-food-cravings-no-desire-for-seafood.3969/post-55244>

I settled for eaitng all the things that would be in my chicken soup minus the chicken. The raw crushed garlic clove I ate though about killed me, burned all the way down. Garlic capsules combined with hot pepper and zicam took care of the cold in a few days rather than the 10 it used to take me to get over something and then fish came back into the menu. Don't understand the fish aversion thing either.

Wow! 10 days with a cold...The best way to beat a cold is with Xlear Nasal Spray made with xylitol and grapefruit seed extract sprayed routinely at onset of twinge symptoms every hour....then 6k to 8k vitamin C and 10k to 50k iu Vit D3 every 2 to 4 hours until symptoms dissappear. Twenty minutes in the hottest water you can stand helps, too. Bam! Knocks it right out. And we're talking hours. Haven't had a full blown miserable cold in 3 or 4 years now and I've got 8 kids. Theirs are few and far between as well. If it starts as a sore throat add Lugol's into the mix. I paint the thyroid and wrists with it. You can also up your intake of it orally as well and of course, Dr. K's fabulous Epi-Paleo diet!

# Thread: Nut cravings
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nut-cravings.3974/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### bigknitwit — 2012-09-27T21:39:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nut-cravings.3974/post-54827>

Why do I crave nuts? Not a day goes by that I don't wish I could eat nuts. Why do I crave them sooo much? I always say if I could just get back nuts and bacon in my diet, I would feel so much better! I can't eat nuts (AI) though. What's creating this unending desire? I eat seafood 1-2X/day...

### ealachan — 2012-09-27T21:54:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nut-cravings.3974/post-54831>

Is there something in the nuts that you're not getting, maybe? Seafood doesn't have EVERYTHING in it that our bodies need. Maybe you're running a deficiency on something else that your body is trying to correct.

### johnnyb — 2012-09-28T07:09:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nut-cravings.3974/post-54938>

I think nuts were a way to get carbs especially on the way tobecame a real fat burner. I think salt need may drive nut craving in me. At this point i get a brazil nut with diner and occasionally some macadamia nuts. Sometimes I wonder if it's not gut flora signaling for them.

### kathiebu@hotmail.com — 2012-09-28T15:21:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nut-cravings.3974/post-54982>

We often are addicted to food we are allergic to... I also crave nuts, but I feel terrible when I have only a few. It wil go by with time I guess.

### nonchalant — 2012-09-28T15:30:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nut-cravings.3974/post-54984>

I think it's the good quality omega 6 fats in nuts.

### prAna303 — 2012-09-28T16:28:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nut-cravings.3974/post-54992>

Is the craving 24/7 and 365? I feel a need to eat them at late autumn when they are available in their natural state where i live (now). In the summer i donÂ´t even have a thought about them. Perhaps your cravings will loosen up the more optimal and seasonal you get.

# Thread: Recipes for Krusing Kids
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/recipes-for-krusing-kids.3978/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 25
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Shijin13 — 2012-09-27T23:06:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/recipes-for-krusing-kids.3978/post-54854>

Ok. Ladies and Gents - for those of us who have small peoples running our lives. Lets drop in their favorite recipes, dishes, sauces etc.

These recipes can be things you make your kiddos or they can be dishes they've come up with on their own, that's made it into the "rotation"

### Shijin13 — 2012-09-27T23:09:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/recipes-for-krusing-kids.3978/post-54855>

Recipe #1: grilled squidies (squid, octopus etc)

marinate whole squid/octopus (small ones frozen from the international market) in a mixture of avocado oil, lime juice, lime zest, garlic, salt, pepper

put on grill (over top of smoker chips)

keep an eye on them... they cook quick. pull off serve, eat and wish you had more and be prepared for the next seafood meal w/o them - you will have a revolt on your hands.

### Jude — 2012-09-28T01:16:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/recipes-for-krusing-kids.3978/post-54886>

Good thread--hoping there will be lots more recipes-have a dil who is trying to implement epi paleo and needs recipes for resistant kids.

### bigknitwit — 2012-09-28T01:48:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/recipes-for-krusing-kids.3978/post-54893>

Not very imaginative, but well received by all 4 of mine: oven roasted salmon. Place salmon skin up (salted) below a cool broiler (I do ours in the toaster oven which isn't as hot as an oven broil, but still top heated), insert meat thermometer and remove when done. My kids love to pull the skin up, eat it separately, and then add more salt to the flesh itself.

### Shijin13 — 2012-09-28T03:44:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/recipes-for-krusing-kids.3978/post-54921>

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/55762=483-ImageUploadedByTapatalk1348792946.792897.jpg)

Not the best pic. But here they are. We buy them frozen. Come 6-8/pack kids go bonkers over then. It's their favorite seafood. I think thy'd eat squidies or octopus at every meal if they could

### Shijin13 — 2012-09-28T16:38:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/recipes-for-krusing-kids.3978/post-54995>

Egg Cups Summer Recipe:

12 eggs beaten

2c Almond Flour (coconut flour doesn't work as well)

1T Baking powder

fat of choice ( I use ghee or coconut oil) ~ 4Tbs sometimes more

vanilla, & cinnamon for flavoring

mix all ingredients, pour into muffin tins - bake at 350 until done

Serve w/ghee These things taste just like french toast! you can add maple syrup or honey if you like - but I don't.

Egg cups (Fall/Winter):

12 eggs beaten

breakfast sausage - you could also sub chopped shrimp or clams or musssels in these too!

chopped onions and garlic

spinach, broccoli, or other winter veggie chopped

cheese (If acceptable)

Fat of Choice

pour into muffin tin and bake at 350. (If you want more of a baked item add 1 cup Almond flour and 1/2T of baking powder)

2 ezpz breakfasts on the go for those crazy mornings and the kids love them!

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-09-28T17:20:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/recipes-for-krusing-kids.3978/post-55003>

ENCHILADAS

When we were transitioning away from grain foods, I made this once a week. It's a great "comfort" type food - and fun to have with company!! Also, if you haven't tried goat cheese cheddar - it's quite awesome. We like it more than the old kind. It's more spendy, but if you just sprinkle on top for the bubbly cheesy brown look, totally worth it and the block will go for a couple of meals.

The other cautionary tale is you must have a damn good non stick pan. The tortillas take a few tries to get you mojo going. You CAN make them in advance, and they freeeze nicely.

I think I got the idea from a Mark Sisson book - as a recovering foodie, I highly advise those for noobs to all of this. When you first start, all this meat/veg can be boring.

Enchiladas!

Wrap

8 egg whites

tbs almond meal if desired, works fine without

1/4 cup of cream or co milk. I have added mct oils all kinds of things to get the texture just right.

Swirl in a small frypan till it is cooked enough to hold together while you flip it. These will get nice brown spots and look awesome in the final product. You just have to mess with this part.

The rest, you can figure out! It's an art not a science!

### BJK77 — 2012-09-29T16:40:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/recipes-for-krusing-kids.3978/post-55204>

Salmon Patties

1 can Wild Alaskan salmon (14.75 oz) - I use one from Trader Joe's that has skin, bones and all!

1 lb fresh Wild Alaskan salmon

2 eggs

1 cooked sweet potato, mashed

Herbs of choice: my kids like dill weed and a little tarragon

1/2 tsp sea salt

Bacon grease or lard

Beat 2 eggs in a large bowl. Cook the fresh salmon and then flake and mix with the canned salmon in the bowl with eggs. Add mashed sweet potato herbs and sea salt. Form into patties and fry in bacon grease or lard.

My kids love these! I've found they also freeze well and they're great to have on hand for quick breakfasts or lunches. Occasionally for a treat or to make them a little different I'll coat the patties in some shredded coconut or chopped macadamia nuts before frying. I use my vitamix to make the macadamias almost into a meal/flour.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-09-30T01:10:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/recipes-for-krusing-kids.3978/post-55303>

> **BJK77;56204 wrote:** Salmon Patties
>
>
>
> 1 can Wild Alaskan salmon (14.75 oz) - I use one from Trader Joe's that has skin, bones and all!
>
> 1 lb fresh Wild Alaskan salmon
>
> 2 eggs
>
> 1 cooked sweet potato, mashed
>
> Herbs of choice: my kids like dill weed and a little tarragon
>
> 1/2 tsp sea salt
>
> Bacon grease or lard
>
>
>
> Beat 2 eggs in a large bowl. Cook the fresh salmon and then flake and mix with the canned salmon in the bowl with eggs. Add mashed sweet potato herbs and sea salt. Form into patties and fry in bacon grease or lard.
>
>
>
> My kids love these! I've found they also freeze well and they're great to have on hand for quick breakfasts or lunches. Occasionally for a treat or to make them a little different I'll coat the patties in some shredded coconut or chopped macadamia nuts before frying. I use my vitamix to make the macadamias almost into a meal/flour.

Love the addition of sweet potato. I'm going to definitely try this.

My kids often don't like seafood that is too fishy, so sauces become really important for us (I do wish Dr K would publish that sauce book!). They like curry sauces (lots of chopped cilantro, mild curry powder, lots of onions, garlic, lemon juice and olive oil) and also olive sauces (I puree olives with garlic, lemon, herbs) as well as pestos (basil pesto being a fave). I find that if I cook the fish in the sauce at a low temp for longer, it absorbs much of the flavour. The sauces work great for shrimp, too.

My son, who is the most sensitive to 'fishy' tastes, prefers his raw. I keep cold smoked salmon on hand and he makes 'sushi bites' almost every day (he invented them and says he should sell them to get rich). He takes a sheet of nori, cuts it into 4 small squares, and puts a piece of cold smoked salmon, a slice of avocado and a slice of pickle in each, along with fresh cilantro or parsley. He then folds and eats. He is 8 and can make this himself, with very little mess or clean up and (best of all for him) no fishy smell.

### BJK77 — 2012-10-01T17:28:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/recipes-for-krusing-kids.3978/post-55577>

Your son sounds like a little budding chef Lauren! I might have to see if my kids want to try out his creation. I have some smoked salmon in the fridge and they're always more likely to try and, more importantly, like something if they're involved in making it.

We made a seafood "cobb" salad for dinner last night that the kids enjoyed, but we're about at the end of the season for some of the ingredients, so we'll have to modify it for winter eating.

I just topped some of our own homegrown lettuce greens with some fresh tomatoes and peppers from our garden along with some sliced mushrooms, avocado, shrimp and crab meat.

I also fried up some bacon to crumble on top and used the grease to make a hot bacon dressing with a little apple cider vinegar, dijon mustard and a touch of honey.

### Shijin13 — 2012-10-01T20:42:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/recipes-for-krusing-kids.3978/post-55628>

Tonight we're making shrimp salad, wrapped in blanched cabbage leaves... haven't decided what we're going to put into it yet... may ask the kids to help season it up!

### Shijin13 — 2012-10-02T02:40:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/recipes-for-krusing-kids.3978/post-55719>

Made these tonight for dinner

[http://www.ibreatheimhungry.com/2012/05/spicy-shrimp-hand-rolls.html](http://http://www.ibreatheimhungry.com/2012/05/spicy-shrimp-hand-rolls.html)

modified w/a "cocktail sauce" the kids requested - as they love horseradish!

topped w/ guacamole, fish roe (we call them fish poppers!), and wrapped them in blanched cabbage leaves!

### Shijin13 — 2012-10-02T20:58:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/recipes-for-krusing-kids.3978/post-55900>

Avocado, seaweed salad, and "fish poppers"

seaweed salad - you can buy pre-made or you can make your own (I've done both)

peel and slice an avocado, shred/crumble seaweed, then scoop out "fish poppers" AKA fish roe (the kids call them fish poppers b/c they pop in their mouths!), top with balsamic vinegar

nom, nom, nom. I've also added this to regular tuna/sardine salad - and Chaos and Havoc love it!

### Shijin13 — 2012-10-03T01:57:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/recipes-for-krusing-kids.3978/post-55979>

Sausage, butternut squash and apples

pre-cook sausage, and butternut squash

throw cooked sausage, butternut squash, and chopped apples into oven proof pan, w/lots of ghee, cinnamon, nutmeg and salt.

put into oven at 350 until it smells awesome, take out and eat.

the more sausage the better!

### Momma28 — 2012-10-03T04:18:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/recipes-for-krusing-kids.3978/post-56001>

Hi Ladies,

This morning's breakfast was sweetpotatoe cassarole with bacon and sunny side up eggs. Sweet potatoe cass. was super easy. Bake pot. and peel. Mash and add eggs and and spices. I used cinn, nutmeg and clove. I added salt, vanilla and stevia glycerite. I blended in a little almond milk as well, topped with walnuts and baked.

Dinner was Mini Meatloaves wrapped in bacon (which were adorable!), broccoli cassarole and left over sweet potatoe. Mini meatloaves was grassfed beef with pureed butternut squash added in for softer texture with eggs and spices. Take about 2 tbls and roll in ball and wrap with 5 in piece of bacon and set on cookie tray. Fill tray and bake at 350 for 45 mins or so. Broccoli cassarole was frozen thawed broccoli with eggs, 1/2 cups mayo and spices of your choice. Bake in same oven with mini meatloaves and serve. The kids loved it!

### Momma28 — 2012-10-03T04:21:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/recipes-for-krusing-kids.3978/post-56003>

Oh, and I forgot last night's fab dinner....Fish Taco Salad... Cod was seasoned with an organic taco seasoning by Frontier and baked till done. Guacamole was made from fresh avocados...a bed of organic mixed greens was placed in a large flat bowl, topped with the fish and some of the juices for dressing and the guac and salsa were added. OMG! Fabulous! Kids loved it and so did hubby! I never missed the cheese or sour cream.

### Momma28 — 2012-10-03T04:32:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/recipes-for-krusing-kids.3978/post-56009>

OK...one more... my kids' and hubby's all-time fav...Meatza! make a "crust" with the ground beef just like you would an italian meatball. Press into bottom of cookie sheet. Bake at 400 for 10 mins. just till light brown. Take out and top with pizza or pasta sauce you make or your choice...add onions, olives, mushrooms...whatever you like...add more seasonings...we LOVE Frontier's Pizza Seasoning...pop back in oven another 30 mins until done and voila! You'll never miss the bread crust and your kids will feel like there is no other pizza in the world. Serve with salad.

### BJK77 — 2012-10-03T17:11:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/recipes-for-krusing-kids.3978/post-56058>

This is a great paleo "chili" for those following the AI protocol and an excellent way to get some good anti-inflammatory spices in the kids! The flavors are so rich that you can also easily hide in some seafood and/or offal to sneak it into squeamish kids

I made it last night and my kids, who generally don’t like anything “spicy,” devoured it in no time! They had it on a bed of homegrown greens and topped it with some homemade raw sour cream.

2 lb ground beef

2 c. beef broth

Â¼ cup tallow

2 c. diced carrots

1 c spinach

2 c. diced zucchini

1/2 c mushroom powder - this was just a powder I blended from mushrooms I dehydrated - you could just add fresh mushrooms

2 T cumin

1.5 T turmeric

1 tsp cloves

2 tsp coriander

2 tsp sea salt

1 T liquid smoke

\*Anything you’d like to sneak in  I added 2 cans of sardines . . . shhh – don’t tell my kids! They’ll eat most kinds of seafood, but I have yet to get my daughter to willingly eat sardines. My son will as long as she isn’t around to make faces and convince him that they are disgusting. Paybacks

\*Note: I’m just estimating the amount of veggies here. I used what I had just gotten fresh out of the garden. I’m sure you could play around with them and still have a delicious meal.

I browned the beef in the tallow while the veggies cooked in the broth. Mixed the two together, added the spices and let it simmer for about an hour to blend the flavors. Easy as can be!

### Shijin13 — 2012-10-03T18:01:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/recipes-for-krusing-kids.3978/post-56064>

> **Momma28;57222 wrote:** OK...one more... my kids' and hubby's all-time fav...Meatza! make a "crust" with the ground beef just like you would an italian meatball. Press into bottom of cookie sheet. Bake at 400 for 10 mins. just till light brown. Take out and top with pizza or pasta sauce you make or your choice...add onions, olives, mushrooms...whatever you like...add more seasonings...we LOVE Frontier's Pizza Seasoning...pop back in oven another 30 mins until done and voila! You'll never miss the bread crust and your kids will feel like there is no other pizza in the world. Serve with salad.

Meatza is always a win. We have it a couple times/month. I've even made it using Sean Conces' recipe from [http://freerange-human.com/2012/03/26/ketogenic-pizza/](http://http://freerange-human.com/2012/03/26/ketogenic-pizza/)

awesome... Just awesome

### Shijin13 — 2012-10-03T18:02:17+0300

# Thread: Back pain help
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/back-pain-help.3979/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### vlynnb — 2012-09-28T01:20:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/back-pain-help.3979/post-54887>

I am posting this for my son who is experiencing pain in his back since starting college this fall and thereby sitting a lot more, hunching over computers, etc. We already know that he has degenerative disk disease and have purchased a consult with Dr. K. but my son has not been able to fit it into his schedule yet.

So, I remember a lot of comments in one or more of the blogs on degenerative dish disease and suggestions for dealing with it but in searching the blogs I can't find anything and alot of the comments are gone now. I particularly remember some pain relievers that were more optimal than others until long term life style, nutrition etc. changes can begin to make a difference. Can anyone help me with references to a particular blog or other info to get some relief?

### BJK77 — 2012-09-28T16:33:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/back-pain-help.3979/post-54993>

I can't help with any short-term pain relievers other than to say CT, CT, CT! My DH has back problems (herniated disc, bulging disc, etc, etc) and he's finding CT to be very effective at preventing pain from setting in as well as relieving it if he overdoes something that causes pain.

### nonchalant — 2012-09-28T17:16:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/back-pain-help.3979/post-55001>

When my back was hurting, icepacks at bedtime helped so much. I got some that lasted 4 hours, they were best.

Other than that, the only thing I would take was bromelain supplements. Oh, and krill oil seem to be just as effective as ibuprophen.

Try to get him to lay off the wheat, it's a killer for DDD. He may notice the pain is worse after that free spaghetti supper or whatever.

### nonchalant — 2012-09-28T17:21:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/back-pain-help.3979/post-55004>

Here's one thread:

[http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?1848-Alternative-anti-inflammatories](http://http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?1848-Alternative-anti-inflammatories)

Hope he gets some relief.

### Jonathin — 2012-09-28T18:29:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/back-pain-help.3979/post-55023>

I am a tall lean guy who has had back problems on and off for years.

Muscle tone is a big issue. Lots of leg lifts will help after a couple of weeks.

Get the ergonomics right with your computer chair and desk height.

For sure supplement with magnesium and possibly with calcium if the diet is poor.

### vlynnb — 2012-09-28T18:55:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/back-pain-help.3979/post-55028>

Thanks so much for your suggestions. I will pass them and the links on to him. He hasn't been reading the blogs or forum but now he is in this much pain I think he will fiind the time. CT will be hard with room mates and only one bathroom in the apartment but even if he can do it once or twice a week and then at home on week-ends I am sure that will help. Winters are sufficiently cold here to get some benefit from walking to classes I think. And he has been open to changing his diet so if he can deal with the pain using the suggestions you gave until the CT, food, and blue light blllockers take effect then he'll be fine, I hope.

### vlynnb — 2012-09-28T19:02:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/back-pain-help.3979/post-55030>

OK, I just checked "What's New" on the forum! Hope my son gets my message before he longs on to this site that I didn't know I was sending him to an objectionable site, that it was previoualy safe to visit.

### nonchalant — 2012-09-28T19:09:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/back-pain-help.3979/post-55034>

Yeah, seems someone found us today.

### stacyj — 2012-09-29T02:56:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/back-pain-help.3979/post-55130>

I have had back pain off and on for several years, worsened dramatically (every day now) after a MVA. I also have neck pain issues as well. For me, it depends on what the pain is from: muscle pain from muscle spasms or just plain tight, clenched muscles; nerve pain- sciatica or radiated pain; or pain from the disks. I have four total herniated disks, several more bulging disks, spondylolisthesis of L5 and L4, plus some torn ligaments in my upper cervical spine.

Ice always helps, heat does too (shhhhhh.....) sometimes just changing positions helps, stretches, going to the chiropractor, and E stim or TENS (one Dr I saw hated this, says it doesnt work- physical therapists all use it and my neurosurgeon finally got me my own unit for home use. Pool therapy feels great- while you are in the pool, lol. Epidural steroid shots can work- didnt work so well for me. I have taken Motrin and muscle relaxants for the last two years- hate to do it , but will as a last resort. Same with narcotics- but all pills are last resort only for me. I just hate taking them.

Definitely clean up his diet, and he has to do something about his posture- nothing will help if he keeps putting his body into positions that are causing the problems.

### kathylu — 2012-09-29T04:59:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/back-pain-help.3979/post-55151>

The biggest thing for me was grain elimination. You have to stick with it strictly for several weeks, but the pain just fades away (of course, then you know you are gluten intolerant). CT helps too, and working on leaky gut issues. Turmeric and fish oil were helpful too. TENS is great for immediate pain relief.

# Thread: stat paging Gretchen
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stat-paging-gretchen.3985/>

**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### KiwiLauren — 2012-09-28T05:42:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stat-paging-gretchen.3985/post-54931>

OK, got a complete set of labs back and it ain't looking pretty. Low cortisol at every measurement point (bottom of all the ranges), low melatonin (just under mid-range), high testosterone (just over the top of the range). And they say high progesterone. And low estrogen, along with poor E metabolism (I'm making more E1 than I should). These labs were drawn without any BHRT. I hadn't started yet. So I have PCOS (without ANY of the symptoms) and my Pg:E2 ratio is fine? And adrenal fatigue, despite being the poster child for circadian obedience. Help. Other than scheduling a consult (done.), any ideas?

### Shijin13 — 2012-09-28T12:54:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stat-paging-gretchen.3985/post-54958>

> **KiwiLauren;55773 wrote:** OK, got a complete set of labs back and it ain't looking pretty. Low cortisol at every measurement point (bottom of all the ranges), low melatonin (just under mid-range), high testosterone (just over the top of the range). And they say high progesterone. And low estrogen, along with poor E metabolism (I'm making more E1 than I should). These labs were drawn without any BHRT. I hadn't started yet. So I have PCOS (without ANY of the symptoms) and my Pg:E2 ratio is fine? And adrenal fatigue, despite being the poster child for circadian obedience. Help. Other than scheduling a consult (done.), any ideas?

Can you post them w/units & ranges? I'm betting something is off. And yes you can have PCOS w/o symptoms. I didnt consider I might have until I talked w/a cousin who's a geneticist she though our entire maternal line had it I never had anyone run a full hormone panel to figure it out

As for you pg:E2 ratio being ok. If your not making e2 or pg yeah I could buy that not sure I want that though. It's what got you where you are in the 1st place

### KiwiLauren — 2012-09-28T22:11:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stat-paging-gretchen.3985/post-55072>

OK, here are the actual figures (by the way, I had a full saliva panel done, which included the ASI w/ melatonin, and P and E1/E2/E3) but on the same day, I also had blood drawn for E2, P, DHEA, hsCRP, thyroid function and D3. So I've got a test for P and E2 that are from different sources but drawn on exactly the same day. Thing is, they are very different. I'll post it all below.)

Saliva Testing:

Cortisol Morning 14.5 (range 6.0-42.0)

Cortisol Afternoon 1.9 (0-15.0)

Cortisol Night 1.8 (0-5.0)

Cortisol Midnight 1.8 (0-5.0)

(note says 'cortisol level is below mean range and suggestive of adrenal insufficiency, suspect adrenal fatigue if all four are below mean range. Suggest supplementation with DHEA and standard adrenal support.)

Melatonin Midnight 19 (0-40)

(note says melatonin is below mean reference range, suggest supplementation with 1-3mg melatonin 30 minutes before bed.)

Estrogens:

E2 Estradiol 10.0 pmol/L (11-29)

E1 Estrone 17.0 (9.6-20)

E3 Estriol 22.0 (0-29.)

E3/E2=E1 ratio 0.81 (>1.0)

P:E2 ratio: 196.6 (4.0-108)

Testosterone 210 (25-190) (note says it is elevated and suggestive of current supplementation with androgen precursors such as testosterone, DHEA or pregnenolone. If not supplemented, then suggestive of PCOS, insulin resistance, fibroids or endometriosis.)

Progesterone 1966 pmol/L (318-1590) (note says it is 'elevated and suggestive of current supplementation')

Blood Testing (same day - both day 20 of cycle)

E2 637 pmol/L (range 160-770)

P 65 (range 15-100)

DHEA 2.9 (.5-12.0)

free T3 3.0 (3.5-6.0)

Free T4 14 (10-24)

TSH 4.7 (.40-4.0)

Tg and TPO antibodies were tested a month ago and came back negative...

Tg 5 iu/ml (

### Shijin13 — 2012-09-29T05:18:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stat-paging-gretchen.3985/post-55152>

Lauren

first think I noticed right off the bat... you're making E3 like crazy (which is the pregnancy hormone & if you check out this link [http://www.thecompounder.com/alternative-treatments/hormone-imbalance/estrogen/the-estrogens-e1-e2-a-e3](http://http://www.thecompounder.com/alternative-treatments/hormone-imbalance/estrogen/the-estrogens-e1-e2-a-e3) it states high levels of E3 are an indication of remission in breast cancer)

Your E2 is on the low end of the range, and your E1 is on the high end. my understanding is E2 is the major driver for Breast Cancer from the Estrogen POV... so having it low is probably a good thing in your case....though you might want to keep an eye on E1 as well b/c that can also drive cancer as well - but I'm sure you know all of this!

Your Pg looks good as does the ratio. I'd keep doing what ever your doing hormone wise - though I think I remember you said you weren't on BHRT yet. is that right? I can't find the other calculator to run the math w/your blood work Pg:E2 #s to see what that ratio looks like.

As for the High T. In my experience taking 100mg of 7keto for 6mths I didn't see a budge in DHEA or T, it was already HIGH. I did see an increase when I started taking the DHEA though (serious out of the ball park #s) the DHEA fed my PCOS (but damn I felt great on the high dose) You can have PCOS w/out all the "traditional symptoms" Don't forget CT can increase T, too. PCOS has both High T and an upside down Pg:E2 ratio (which can be a direct contributor to BC - I don't know if you have labs from prior to the BC dx and during - but if you did it would be interesting to see what things looked like then)

Your TSH indicates you're probably HYPO, your T3 is low, and your T4 is middle of the road - your T4 is probably being converted to rT3 (which I think you said getting the test is like leading a horse to water and getting it to drink!)

Your adrenals are in the Tank, but that's probably b/c your T4->rT3 instead of T3. As for your adrenals, they're shot at the Brain (BG12) most likely b/c of LR, you probably have a higher BG Through out the day vs your fasting BG - only way to know is to get a monitor and test occasionally to see where things are at. Other impacts on the adrenals are the BC and your recent earthquakes - you're body's 100% in Survival mode - but what makes it interesting is your still making some hormones, and at a pretty good clip. based on the ASI I would say you're in the survival chain instead of the progesterone, except - can't be 100% sure b/c you don't have a VAP/NMR lipid panel & the fact your making some hormones. doesn't seem right - wonder if that hormone production is part of the remenats of the BC? (Thinking out loud here - ie cells, want to live not die whole thing)

Vit D is great considering you're coming out of winter!

you've still got some inflammation going on considering your hsCRP. the question is why? What's driving it? I'd say go back and re-read BG12 again.... and pay attention to the impact of iodine, and DHA. then really dig into your hormones, and figure out what's going on... definitely get a Lipid panel, and an rT3 if you can find a lab who'll pull it..

also... might be worth while to PM Jansz and Quelsen and email Colleen Coble - they're all really good at looking at labs.

that's a quick look... I'll try to come back and see if I might have missed anything tomorrow morning... great talking this evening w/you and the ladies!

### KiwiLauren — 2012-09-29T06:15:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stat-paging-gretchen.3985/post-55162>

Gretchen, you are a star. I need to go eat my dinner (MHS and raw roe on the menu so I can start to think like you!), but wanted to give you my old lipid panel. It's 6+ months old now (done March of this year) in case it helps. Your insights re: the different hormonal processes are very interesting. Of course it's a complex picture with the tie-in to BC. I can't imagine how I got my P so high without any BHRT. I wonder, could I have gotten BC without an upside down Pg-E2 ratio? I don't have any pre-BC labs. I didn't know anything back then and of course none of my docs had a clue either. Thank you so much. Lauren

lipid panel from 3/2102...

cholesterol is 313

Trigs are 62

HDL is 84

LDL is 216 (I do not have a breakdown between small and large)

# Thread: Cannibals
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cannibals.3987/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Michael — 2012-09-28T08:39:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cannibals.3987/post-54944>

Here is an article from Chris Masterjohn entitled:

**Weston Price Looked for Vegans But Found Only Cannibals

Back in 1935, Price visited some remote South Sea islands. He found there were basically two types of food consumed on the islands: plant foods (including roots, tubers and fruits) and seafood (including hard- and soft-shelled, and invertebrates, together with fish of various types).

Price was interested to see if people in the interior of the islands, who therefore did not have access to the sea, would be subsisting entirely on plants. (Hence Chris's title.)

In fact, this didn't happen, because there was trade. This is one reason why I thought this interesting to post, because it recalls Maggie's comment in an earlier thread that people can trade food:

> He informed me also that even during the periods of bitter warfare the people from the mountain district would come down to the sea during the night and place in caches delicious plants which grew only at the higher altitudes. They would return the following night to obtain the sea foods that were placed in the same caches by the people from the sea. He stated that even during warfare these messengers would not be captured or disturbed.
>
>
> But that's not all. This will be of interest from the epi-paleo point of view, too:
>
>
>
> This guide and many others explained to me that cannibalism had its origin in the recognition by the hill people that the livers and other organs of their enemies from the coast provided the much needed chemicals which were requisite to supplement the plant foods. ...
>
>
>
> ... fishermen, were especially sought for

**

> staying a famine. One native told me of having left an Island where he was engaged in fishing because of a tip that came to him that his life was in danger because of his occupation.

He was a fisherman, and they were coming for him. Yikes!

[http://www.westonaprice.org/blogs/cmasterjohn/2011/05/12/weston-price-looked-for-vegans-but-found-only-cannibals/](http://http://www.westonaprice.org/blogs/cmasterjohn/2011/05/12/weston-price-looked-for-vegans-but-found-only-cannibals/)

### indigogirl — 2012-09-28T17:06:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cannibals.3987/post-54999>

The aboriginal form of supplementation!

Really interesting.

### nonchalant — 2012-09-28T17:26:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cannibals.3987/post-55006>

> **Michael;55806 wrote:**
>
> He was a fisherman, and they were coming for him. Yikes!

So what you're saying, Michael, is that we shouldn't purchase any Jack Kruse TShirts?

### Michael — 2012-09-28T17:30:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cannibals.3987/post-55007>

> **Nonchalant;55923 wrote:** So what you're saying, Michael, is that we shouldn't purchase any Jack Kruse TShirts?

# Thread: Is it getting a cold,  or your reaction to getting it?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-it-getting-a-cold-or-your-reaction-to-getting-it.4000/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-09-29T00:11:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-it-getting-a-cold-or-your-reaction-to-getting-it.4000/post-55095>

I know what I think the answer is - but I am always enlightened by the comments on here.

So, since we are all gettin so optimal, should our ramped up immune functions protect us from simply getting the virus (say a cold) or will it also help out if we go ahead and get one and make it much less obnoxious?

In theory , it should hop into action and make us a wheezing mess - but for a short time right?

### Souldanzer — 2012-09-29T00:29:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-it-getting-a-cold-or-your-reaction-to-getting-it.4000/post-55101>

Well I was just thinking about some of that. You know how people on here recommend to take mega doses of vitamin D when you feel a cold coming on...... well, let's just say it's true (and I think it is) that vitamin D technically is a hormone, not a nutrient, but a steroid hormone that has anti-inflammatory action..... who says the mega dose of D is actually helping the eradicate the cold by ramping up the immune system..... you could also say that the mega dose of the steroid hormone acted like an anti-inflammatory, let's say ibuprofen, you know what happens if you take ibuprofen when you feel like crap from a cold? You feel better. But you still got the cold. It's just that it's just disabled your immune system's action.

That would also mean that your hs CRP would drop with vitamin D mega doses? I feel like I'm missing something important here in my thinking.

### indigogirl — 2012-09-29T00:38:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-it-getting-a-cold-or-your-reaction-to-getting-it.4000/post-55103>

Really not sure, but why suffer?

Colds ( when I rarely get them ) tend to go into my chest so I do everything I can to stop or at least lessen their impact.

### Souldanzer — 2012-09-29T00:46:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-it-getting-a-cold-or-your-reaction-to-getting-it.4000/post-55105>

> **indigogirl;56061 wrote:** Really not sure, but why suffer?
>
> Colds ( when I rarely get them ) tend to go into my chest so I do everything I can to stop or at least lessen their impact.

Not sure if you're asking me here.... but if you did, I feel like I'm off somewhere so take this for what it's worth. It would be counter-indicated b/c you would be down-regulating your innate immune system. So actually, you would make the suffering worse in the end. By feeling less of the cold for a while, you prevent your body from attacking the virus.

### nonchalant — 2012-09-29T00:49:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-it-getting-a-cold-or-your-reaction-to-getting-it.4000/post-55106>

I've not had a virus for quite a while, but I'd like to see what CT is like when you're sick. Is it unbearable? Is it pleasant?

### indigogirl — 2012-09-29T00:54:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-it-getting-a-cold-or-your-reaction-to-getting-it.4000/post-55107>

> **Souldanzer;56068 wrote:** Not sure if you're asking me here.... but if you did, I feel like I'm off somewhere so take this for what it's worth. It would be counter-indicated b/c you would be down-regulating your innate immune system. So actually, you would make the suffering worse in the end. By feeling less of the cold for a while, you prevent your body from attacking the virus.

Soul, were you replying to me?

In the past if I have gotten a cold or an upper respiratory infection after trying to prevent it, it didn't seem to be as bad as everyone else's, but this actually could have nothing to do with my trying to prevent it!

### prAna303 — 2012-09-29T00:58:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-it-getting-a-cold-or-your-reaction-to-getting-it.4000/post-55109>

> **Nonchalant;56069 wrote:** I've not had a virus for quite a while, but I'd like to see what CT is like when you're sick. Is it unbearable? Is it pleasant?

When i was a young child my parents always forced me to play in the cold (winter is our season for colds) without any protection gear or clothing. Simply just put me outside and let me in when dinner was ready... Did i suffer? No, i did not think that much about beeing sick, just having fun. Today i still feel the same when i get a little notion about getting a cold, get out and let nature have itÂ´s action on you, mostly it will be over in a couple of hours of sleep.

### Shijin13 — 2012-09-29T00:59:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-it-getting-a-cold-or-your-reaction-to-getting-it.4000/post-55110>

> **Nonchalant;56069 wrote:** I've not had a virus for quite a while, but I'd like to see what CT is like when you're sick. Is it unbearable? Is it pleasant?

It's both at least in my case last spring. It would help me get to sleep but I'd cough my butt of and wake myself up. I did feel better about an hr after CT

# Thread: Website Build Update + instructions for consults
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/website-build-update-instructions-for-consults.4004/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### tellmisty — 2012-09-29T03:48:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/website-build-update-instructions-for-consults.4004/post-55139>

Hi everyone,

Whew, what a week! Thank you so much for being so patient with this big move ... overall, you've been really great about it and we are excited about how this is all coming together!

Darren has been working is arse off ironing out the login kinks. It looks like most of you are able to log in with no problems, but if for some reason, you're still having issues, [open a support ticket](http://https://jackkruse.zendesk.com/home) and we'll get it taken care of pronto.

Our next two big transitions are getting the consult process fully automated online, and porting over the forums. Automating the consult process is a bit tricky because we are, frankly, making it kick ass.  We're building in functionality to automate sending in your labs and uploading your health history to your profile, plus scheduling on Dr. Kruse's calendar (bye-bye Tungle; upgrading to a more reliable program). The tracking will be superb.

SO. In the meantime, if you need to book a consult that you've already paid for:

\[list type=decimal\]
\[\*\]Open a support ticket to get access to Dr. Kruse's calendar
\[\*\]Attach your medical disclaimer if this is your first consult to the support ticket ([download it here](http://http://d11wbd7088zyei.cloudfront.net/wp-content/uploads/2012/06/Consult-DISCLAIMER.pdf))
Attach your labs to the support ticket
You will need to plan on calling Jack via Skype for your consult. His Skype ID is jack.kruse69
\[/list type=decimal\]

If you are a Silver or Gold member and want an email lab consult ($99 for Silver, free for Gold), hang tight. I'm expecting that you'll be able to do that first of next week.

You should be able to download any webinars at this point when you login. If you are having any issues at all, open a support ticket and Darren or I will take care of you.

Thanks, everyone!

Misty

### Jude — 2012-10-03T02:26:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/website-build-update-instructions-for-consults.4004/post-55983>

Hi Misty,

There are no " comments" available for BG 12 & 13 when I log in.

# Thread: how bad are commercial eggs and how much is safe to consumed?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-bad-are-commercial-eggs-and-how-much-is-safe-to-consumed.4015/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### x_danny_x — 2012-09-29T16:44:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-bad-are-commercial-eggs-and-how-much-is-safe-to-consumed.4015/post-55205>

okay, pastured/free range eggs are the way to go, but not everyone can afford them on a daily basis. in fact, i just buy cage free eggs that are mainly fed grains to the chicken but at least no antibiotics, steroids, etc are given to the chickens (well that really depends if you are buying from a trusty source)

but even cage free eggs can hurt your wallet. commercial eggs are cheap but the least beneficial out of the eggs. how much can you consume from these guys. i know they are mainly fed corn and have almost no omega 3s content.

do the steroids, antiboitics or whatever else unatural things gets done to the chicken gets passed down to the egg?

### Endless — 2012-09-29T17:35:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-bad-are-commercial-eggs-and-how-much-is-safe-to-consumed.4015/post-55218>

I'd opt for eating less eggs and buying the higher quality ones, personally...

### indigogirl — 2012-09-29T20:36:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-bad-are-commercial-eggs-and-how-much-is-safe-to-consumed.4015/post-55249>

Danny, I've always assumed they get passed down to eggs. Does what a mother eats affect her fetus? Yes!

# Thread: Inflammation reduction, give me the barebones of it!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/inflammation-reduction-give-me-the-barebones-of-it.4018/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Endless — 2012-09-29T19:46:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/inflammation-reduction-give-me-the-barebones-of-it.4018/post-55240>

I KNOW that all of this info is out there in the various blog posts....I am trying to read and re-read and assimilate all I can, and also working on getting my labs done so I can have a consult.

In the meantime....what are the most important things to focus on for reducing inflammation? I intuitively feel that I have a lot of it, but I'm not sure what to focus on the most right now. I am following AI as I suspect Hashimotos as well.

Somewhere there was a reference to using Bitter Melon for CT...why? I bought some but am unsure why or how to use it.

### kathylu — 2012-09-30T15:47:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/inflammation-reduction-give-me-the-barebones-of-it.4018/post-55377>

Take bitter melon 90 minutes before CT. It encourages the conversion of WAT to BAT. No one knows the exact mechanism.

# Thread: Tomorrow is My Last Vitamin D Sun Day this Year
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tomorrow-is-my-last-vitamin-d-sun-day-this-year.4019/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 17
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Huck — 2012-09-29T20:37:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tomorrow-is-my-last-vitamin-d-sun-day-this-year.4019/post-55250>

According to the [USNO](http://http://aa.usno.navy.mil/data/docs/AltAz.php), September 30 is the last day this year the sun is at least 50 degrees of altitude at 12 noon at my location. According to Dr Mercola's site, the sun has to be 50 degrees or higher to get any vitamin d from the sun. 98% overcast today and 50% tomorrow, so tomorrow is my last shot this year. I did get about 2 hours of sun yesterday and another 3 hours on Thursday.

I increased my vitamin d supplements this week and plan on retesting in a couple of weeks.

### nonchalant — 2012-09-30T02:41:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tomorrow-is-my-last-vitamin-d-sun-day-this-year.4019/post-55319>

It's interesting that one can get a suntan in Winter, though, at least down south. But in October the sun's altitude goes below 50deg.

### Huck — 2012-09-30T17:07:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tomorrow-is-my-last-vitamin-d-sun-day-this-year.4019/post-55388>

> **Nonchalant;56327 wrote:** It's interesting that one can get a suntan in Winter, though, at least down south. But in October the sun's altitude goes below 50deg.

At least you get several more weeks of "good" sun than I do.

It is 100% overcast again today, so no sun for me. I'm thinking I might do a road trip early this week chasing the sun for 1 last fill-up.

### AKMan — 2012-09-30T20:22:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tomorrow-is-my-last-vitamin-d-sun-day-this-year.4019/post-55439>

Hey, that's a really neat website, thanks, Huck!

Want to see what I'm up against? Type in Fairbanks, AK. Look at dates 21 June and 21 Dec. On 21 June, when the sun should be at it's highest (Summer Solstice), it only gets to 48 degrees--but it's above zero from 2am til 1130pm. On Dec 21st (Winter Solstice), the sun only hits 2.1 degrees! And it's only above zero from 11am til 2:30pm.

That said, I have the best suntan I've ever had and my Vit D is 58 w/no supps since April. I sat shirtless in the sun for 20-30 minutes every day around noon.

### shilohman — 2012-09-30T20:52:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tomorrow-is-my-last-vitamin-d-sun-day-this-year.4019/post-55450>

Excuse me for being cynical, but the statement that the sun has to be 50 degrees or higher to get vitamin D doesn't make any sense. Perhaps the amount of vitamin D produced drops off as the sun shifts its position during the fall/winter, but stops completely, I don't think so. Does Dr. Mercola by any chance sell vitamin D supplements?

### Huck — 2012-09-30T21:45:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tomorrow-is-my-last-vitamin-d-sun-day-this-year.4019/post-55463>

> **shilohman;56484 wrote:** Excuse me for being cynical, but the statement that the sun has to be 50 degrees or higher to get vitamin D doesn't make any sense. Perhaps the amount of vitamin D produced drops off as the sun shifts its position during the fall/winter, but stops completely, I don't think so. Does Dr. Mercola by any chance sell vitamin D supplements?

> Due to the physics and wavelength of UVB rays, they will only penetrate the atmosphere when the sun is above an angle of about 50Â° from the horizon. When the sun is lower than 50Â°, the ozone layer reflects the UVB-rays but let through the longer UVA-rays.

[http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2012/09/29/sun-exposure-vitamin-d-production-benefits.aspx?e\_cid=20120929\_DNL\_art\_2](http://http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2012/09/29/sun-exposure-vitamin-d-production-benefits.aspx?e_cid=20120929_DNL_art_2)

### DarleenMB — 2012-10-01T01:17:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tomorrow-is-my-last-vitamin-d-sun-day-this-year.4019/post-55494>

My PA told me pretty much the same thing. Tomorrow at noon we're at 41.6. Those tanning beds are looking pretty good right about now.

### Joann — 2012-10-01T01:35:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tomorrow-is-my-last-vitamin-d-sun-day-this-year.4019/post-55501>

When I use this calculator, it says I can still get Vitamin D at my location.

[http://nadir.nilu.no/~olaeng/fastrt/VitD-ez\_quartMED.html](http://http://nadir.nilu.no/~olaeng/fastrt/VitD-ez_quartMED.html)

### nonchalant — 2012-10-01T22:27:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tomorrow-is-my-last-vitamin-d-sun-day-this-year.4019/post-55655>

Dr. K says to get sun every day, even when it's cloudy. So that's what I'm doing.

That, and taking 15k IU Vitamin D daily...

### AndrÃ©s — 2012-10-01T22:54:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tomorrow-is-my-last-vitamin-d-sun-day-this-year.4019/post-55665>

### shilohman — 2012-10-02T02:59:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tomorrow-is-my-last-vitamin-d-sun-day-this-year.4019/post-55728>

> **Huck;56497 wrote:** From a recent Mercola article.

>
>

>
>
> [http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2012/09/29/sun-exposure-vitamin-d-production-benefits.aspx?e\_cid=20120929\_DNL\_art\_2](http://http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2012/09/29/sun-exposure-vitamin-d-production-benefits.aspx?e_cid=20120929_DNL_art_2)

My understanding is that its Ultraviolet light from the sun that causes our bodies to produce vitamin D. The Mercola article implies that we only UVB is what causes this and not UVA. Wonder how AKMAN got is suntan? Maybe it was the UVA.

### Huck — 2012-10-02T04:28:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tomorrow-is-my-last-vitamin-d-sun-day-this-year.4019/post-55753>

>
>
>
> the ultraviolet frequencies responsible for tanning are often divided into the UVA and UVB ranges

[http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sun\_tanning#Tanning\_process](http://http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sun_tanning#Tanning_process)

### AndrÃ©s — 2012-10-02T08:07:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tomorrow-is-my-last-vitamin-d-sun-day-this-year.4019/post-55773>

There is a diference between "lots of vitamin D" when the sun is high in the sky (8,000IU in less than 15 minutes of full body exposure) and "some vitamin D" when it is not so high, such your shade is between once and twice as long as you are (less than 5,000UI in 1 hour exposure if near twice).

"50 degrees" is a useless oversimplification, Ola Engelsen program is not.

### Joann — 2012-10-02T12:13:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tomorrow-is-my-last-vitamin-d-sun-day-this-year.4019/post-55781>

my understanding is it depends on where you live. year round vit d if you are at 35 degrees or higher.

### Joann — 2012-10-02T21:51:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tomorrow-is-my-last-vitamin-d-sun-day-this-year.4019/post-55921>

So yesterday I went out with my D minder app.....107 degrees ...yuk not overcast. I got 0 vitamin d since I went out too late. I just now was out for 10 minutes ....the app showed a little over 4000 iu's of vitamin d. elevation angle 50.9. it a 100 degrees outside. I had to come inside, starting to feel ill from the blazing sun.

I love this app....it has a timer, with a minimum and maximum time to get vit d and not sunburn.

# Thread: CO and epipaleo
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/co-and-epipaleo.4023/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### cookworm — 2012-09-30T05:23:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/co-and-epipaleo.4023/post-55344>

Is it still a good idea to include coconut oil while going more epipaleo?

### indigogirl — 2012-09-30T05:35:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/co-and-epipaleo.4023/post-55346>

Cookworm, it all depends upon your context as Jack would say.

To better absorb the DHA from seafood one should eat seafood with MUFAS.

As we go into fall and winter we should not use coconut oil as it is a tropical oil.

However, if you are really "broken" or obese I think coconut oil should still be used.

It really depends upon your needs.

# Thread: Crispy Fish Skin...the new bacon!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/crispy-fish-skin-the-new-bacon.4025/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 19
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### kathylu — 2012-09-30T15:42:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/crispy-fish-skin-the-new-bacon.4025/post-55376>

I remember having crispy fish skin rolls in sushi restaurants and thinking how yummy they were, but I had never tried to make one before. So I thought, "Why not?"

Went to the asian fish market, had them filet some fish for dinner for me and asked them to package up the heads, bones (of course!) and the skin. They didn't even blink!

Rinse the fish skin and dry it on paper towels, then fry it in bacon fat until crisp. Sprinkle with a little salt, eat and enjoy! It was crisp and delicious, very close to bacon in flavor, not "fishy" at all. I'm sure you could use coconut oil too.

The entire family liked it. Try it.

### nonchalant — 2012-09-30T16:30:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/crispy-fish-skin-the-new-bacon.4025/post-55381>

Thanks for the idea, kathylu. I haven't tried it in bacon fat. Mmmm.

### SeaHorse — 2012-09-30T16:43:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/crispy-fish-skin-the-new-bacon.4025/post-55383>

One of the online fish stores...I think it might have been the one Jack suggested sells crispy fish skins for PETS!!! They may not scale the skin, but I think there is a goldmine in the concept..."Fish Crisps"

Salmon skin is particularly yummy in bacon fat.

### diane — 2012-09-30T16:56:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/crispy-fish-skin-the-new-bacon.4025/post-55386>

This is yet another amazing thread from a while back. This time I was merely hunting for ideas about what to do with salmon skin. When raw, it is as tough as leather. # Thread: Cod Liver Oil, Vtamin Butter Cod Liver Oil Blend....or Emulsified Cod Liver Oil
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cod-liver-oil-vtamin-butter-cod-liver-oil-blend-or-emulsified-cod-liver-oil.4027/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Raiken3712 — 2012-09-30T17:25:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cod-liver-oil-vtamin-butter-cod-liver-oil-blend-or-emulsified-cod-liver-oil.4027/post-55390>

[http://www.greenpasture.org/public/Products/EmulsifiedCodLiverOil/index.cfm](http://http://www.greenpasture.org/public/Products/EmulsifiedCodLiverOil/index.cfm)

[http://www.greenpasture.org/public/Products/CodLiverOil/index.cfm](http://http://www.greenpasture.org/public/Products/CodLiverOil/index.cfm)

[http://www.greenpasture.org/public/Products/ButterCodLiverBlend/index.cfm](http://http://www.greenpasture.org/public/Products/ButterCodLiverBlend/index.cfm)

I've been thinking about getting some Cod Liver oil....but I'm not sure which one is best. I figure its a cheaper way than buying enough wild caught fish. If I had the money I would buy my fill of wild caught fish and other seafood. I just don't have it right now. Extra costs at the moment don't help.

Which of these supplements to help raise levels is the best option? I'm guessing the Butter oil blend isn't good because of the saturated fat blocking absorption of the PUFA?

I looked up emulsification because I didn't remember exactly what it was....and I'm not at all clear which one is better. Also I was thinking of the Mint supplement....but would it be better to get Cinnamon or unflavored?

Or perhaps I would be better off getting another supplement? I'm mostly looking for Iodine because of Thyroid issues. The Selenium, Vitamin D....and others is an added plus. Also I'm wondering how nasty this stuff is...any tips on taking it...I've heard it can be pretty bad. Should I take the standard dose or twice that?

I guess something else that might help is my [Omega 3/6 Assay](http://http://www.keepandshare.com/doc/4638949/redacted-bloodspot-fatty-acid-profile-pdf-183k?da=y)

I'm also curious about the Vitamin D/A antagonism. This works in a similar way as the PUFA/SAFA antagonism? Would the body not absorb some of it based on what it needs? Or do you absorb very little of the A and D because they fight for absorption...

It makes sense to me that you would absorb something....as just because two things are fighting for receptors shouldn't stop either one from being used at all.....also what prevents them from being used afterwards.

If say the Vitamin D is absorbed and the body has no room to absorb anymore Vitamin D or A I guess the body gets rid of the excess before it can be used?

### hope2conceive — 2012-09-30T18:16:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cod-liver-oil-vtamin-butter-cod-liver-oil-blend-or-emulsified-cod-liver-oil.4027/post-55404>

Hi, have you checked out Weston A Price? You can find more info on a lot of your questions in these linked articles:

[http://www.westonaprice.org/cod-liver-oil](http://http://www.westonaprice.org/cod-liver-oil)

CLO is an amazing superfood based on the research of Weston A Price. I'm not sure how much Dr K thinks of it but it can do some really good things. There are testimonials all over these pages.

As far as the butter oil, Price found that a Vitamin K source, such as butter oil, was needed for absorption of Vitamin A and D in the CLO which is the main goal for using CLO though the benefits are so varied. From the site:

Because it is so difficult to obtain adequate fat-soluble activators in the modern diet, Dr. Price recommended cod liver oil to provide vitamins A and D, along with a source of vitamin K \[which Dr Price used to call Activator x\], such as butter from grass-fed animals or what he called high-vitamin butter oil, made by low-temperature centrifuging of butter from cows eating rapidly growing grass. Consumed in liberal amounts during pregnancy, lactation and the period of growth, these nutrients ensure the optimal physical and mental development of children; consumed by adults, these nutrients protect against acute and chronic disease.

### x_danny_x — 2012-09-30T19:35:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cod-liver-oil-vtamin-butter-cod-liver-oil-blend-or-emulsified-cod-liver-oil.4027/post-55421>

Cod Liver Oil better than Fish Oil???

### Michael — 2012-09-30T20:03:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cod-liver-oil-vtamin-butter-cod-liver-oil-blend-or-emulsified-cod-liver-oil.4027/post-55428>

> **Raiken3712;56418 wrote:** [http://www.greenpasture.org/public/Products/EmulsifiedCodLiverOil/index.cfm](http://http://www.greenpasture.org/public/Products/EmulsifiedCodLiverOil/index.cfm)
>
> [http://www.greenpasture.org/public/Products/CodLiverOil/index.cfm](http://http://www.greenpasture.org/public/Products/CodLiverOil/index.cfm)
>
> [http://www.greenpasture.org/public/Products/ButterCodLiverBlend/index.cfm](http://http://www.greenpasture.org/public/Products/ButterCodLiverBlend/index.cfm)
>
>
>
> I've been thinking about getting some Cod Liver oil....but I'm not sure which one is best. I figure its a cheaper way than buying enough wild caught fish. If I had the money I would buy my fill of wild caught fish and other seafood. I just don't have it right now. Extra costs at the moment don't help.
>
>
>
> Which of these supplements to help raise levels is the best option?

I think Jack's preference would be to eat the whole cod liver. That's what he has linked from his site:

[http://jackkruse.com/recommended-products/](http://http://jackkruse.com/recommended-products/)

You can see "Rugenfisch Cod Liver in Own Juice and Oil" there.

I'd guess that's because it's coming as the full package that way and you get iodine, etc with it that way.

The green pastures CLO might be a reasonable option, though, since it is fermented not processed with heat. I don't know if he's commented on it specifically. I would stay away from the one that's emulsified with mint oil - I had a bottle of that and when I wasn't very far down the bottle it stopped coming out. It turned out it had set solid. I don't know if it was the emulsification process that had caused that, but once bitten twice shy. I have had the one called Oslo Orange and that was OK.

### Raiken3712 — 2012-09-30T21:05:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cod-liver-oil-vtamin-butter-cod-liver-oil-blend-or-emulsified-cod-liver-oil.4027/post-55451>

Have any clue what the cost comparison is? How much would eating that Cod Liver cost to equal the same number of servings as the Cod Liver Oil. My decision of the Cod Liver oil is based on cost per serving and ease of use.

Non emulsified is 118 servings is 37 cents a serving at 44.00. 118 servings if taken one a day is 4 months or so. Emulsified seems to have less servings...not sure of the advantage or disadvantage. I don't even see anything on their page for why you would want one over the other.

As far as the Butter oil...I use Kerrygold Irish Butter so figure I can get my K from that. I'm just not sure about the whole issue with absorption of PUFA with SAFA. Jack mentioned that we shouldn't eat saturated fats with fish as I think he said PUFA and SAFA compete for absorption....similar to the Vitamin D/A...not sure.

Thanks for the input.

### Michael — 2012-09-30T21:34:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cod-liver-oil-vtamin-butter-cod-liver-oil-blend-or-emulsified-cod-liver-oil.4027/post-55460>

I guess once you open the tin you eat the whole thing rather than leaving it sitting round, though I guess you could share the contents. But I assume that's a pretty fair hit of what it has to deliver and you don't need to do it that often.
>
>
>
> The 118 servings will be based on half a metric teaspoon a day, as that's the suggested dose.
>
>
>
> I don't know. I guess if one can a week would deliver what 1/2 tsp a day does, the cost difference wouldn't be much. Whether it does I don't know.

# Thread: Eating Fish 3 Meals A Day for A While ... Good Idea?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eating-fish-3-meals-a-day-for-a-while-good-idea.4037/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### RebelLibertarian — 2012-10-01T01:22:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eating-fish-3-meals-a-day-for-a-while-good-idea.4037/post-55495>

Howdy all, anyone see any problems with consuming 90%+ of my animal protein in the form of whole small fish (sardines and the like) and mussels? (Looking to buy frozen, in bulk, but don't want any iodine or selenium toxicities! I should probably add a source of O-6 fats) The rest would be from pastured eggs and GF beef organ meats.

### nonchalant — 2012-10-01T04:55:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eating-fish-3-meals-a-day-for-a-while-good-idea.4037/post-55522>

Welcome, Rebel!

Occasionally I eat seafood for all 3 meals. More often I eat it for 2 meals. And sometimes I'll just get one epi-meal per day. I do often eat bacon and eggs.

I'm not sure what you mean by iodine or selenium toxicities. Those are both excellent minerals to stock up on.

### Michael — 2012-10-01T09:30:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eating-fish-3-meals-a-day-for-a-while-good-idea.4037/post-55541>

> **RebelLibertarian;56566 wrote:** ... but don't want any iodine or selenium toxicities!
>
>
> Might happen if you were popping pills I guess. If you're eating seafood so that these nutrients come "in their evolutionary packages" as Jack would put it, it's hard to see how they'd be likely to cause problems. Fishermen aren't known to run into any specific problems from eating largely of their nearest available food-source that I know of.
>
>
>
> Last I heard WHO was saying that two _billion_ people worldwide were iodine-deficient. Currently, it says on their site:
>
>
>
> Iodine deficiency is the world’s most prevalent, yet easily preventable, cause of brain damage.

[http://www.who.int/nutrition/topics/idd/en/](http://http://www.who.int/nutrition/topics/idd/en/)

Their strategy is to iodinate salt - and that's helping even though it's not delivering iodine in the "evolutionary package".

I find it difficult to believe that some shrimp and sole and whatnot are going to cause anyone a problem. The onus of proof would be on anyone who should claim that: it would need to be shown - and with cast-iron data and huge data sets to be believable, because it just doesn't make sense.

### EllieHarrison — 2012-10-01T15:09:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eating-fish-3-meals-a-day-for-a-while-good-idea.4037/post-55552>

When my cousin started doing the last 4 weeks before a comp on fish he came in a lot better, and he chose a reasonably oily fish as well, as he had dropped his carbs right down by that stage. When he did this, he won my last 4 comps in a row, as compared to using chicken etc, which seemed hit and miss for him. Always ate red meat and whole eggs in the off season though.

Ellie

\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_

Lifespanmeds.com

### RebelLibertarian — 2012-10-02T02:40:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eating-fish-3-meals-a-day-for-a-while-good-idea.4037/post-55720>

Thanks, guys! I'll report back in 10 weeks, whereupon I should have exhausted my supply of sardines

# Thread: How much sea veggies?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-sea-veggies.4039/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BJK77 — 2012-10-01T02:13:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-sea-veggies.4039/post-55505>

I am finding myself really drawn to sea veggies lately. I've been incorporating more of them into my diet since the epi-paleo blog came out, but lately I'm wanting TONS of them and they're really the only veggie I want other than a bit of avocado or mushrooms.

I follow Terry Wahls on FB and lately she's been putting up quite a few posts about being careful to not overdo seaweed, so I'm wondering how much is too much? How much have you all been eating? I don't measure my food out anymore, but I'm sure I'm eating about 50-100 grams of dried sea veg/day.

### ealachan — 2012-10-01T14:57:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-sea-veggies.4039/post-55550>

I think it might depend a lot on your individual situation, like so many other things. I've started eating kelp every day - I toast a roughly 5X5 square of it and the break it up into chips to have with my tuna at lunch time. It's tasty, and the crunch makes a nice counterpoint to the soft tuna. I find it super salty, though...I don't know if I could eat as much as you're eating without feeling like I was going to explode.

### nonchalant — 2012-10-01T17:24:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-sea-veggies.4039/post-55573>

I have yet to get into sea veggies much. But I've started drinking Inger's water, and adding a bit of seaweed here and there to my food. I hope I become like you, BJK77, and really start to crave them. They don't taste bad, I just don't think about using them.

### x_danny_x — 2012-10-02T08:27:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-sea-veggies.4039/post-55775>

last time i checked avocado is a fruit, not a veggie.

### Michael — 2012-10-02T09:29:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-sea-veggies.4039/post-55778>

> **BJK77;56576 wrote:
>
> I follow Terry Wahls on FB and lately she's been putting up quite a few posts about being careful to not overdo seaweed, so I'm wondering how much is too much?
>
>
> Does she say why, BJK77?
>
>
>
> I don't do Facebook, but I tried googling "terry wahls seaweed" out of interest and found this:
>
>
>
> [http://www.terrywahls.com/ForumRetrieve.aspx?ForumID=3749&TopicID=12314](http://http://www.terrywahls.com/ForumRetrieve.aspx?ForumID=3749&TopicID=12314)
>
>
>
> There all she says is:
>
>
>
>
> > Easiest way to begin using seaweed is to buy dulse flakes (health food store, Asian store) and add as a spice to what ever you are cooking. Start small. Try a 1/4 teaspoon at first. It is lovely addition to soups, stews, roasts, vegetables or even sprinkled on a salad. Do not exceed 1/4 tsp per person per day however. If you are taking thyroid medicine - adding seaweed to your diet can impact the amount of thyroid medication that you need.
>
>
>
> There's an apparent contradiction there, since she says start with a 1/4 of a teaspoon, then says "Do not exceed 1/4 tsp". I guess she meant don't exceed that at first.
>
>
>
> There the notion of "too much" seems to be tied to a "too soon". But she gives no rationale for that.
>
>
>
> The final sentence there is a bombshell**:
>
>
>
> If you are taking thyroid medicine - adding seaweed to your diet can impact the amount of thyroid medication that you need.

It's said almost offhand, but what it seems to amount to is putting a pretty large question mark against current medical practice. At least that's how things seem to me. I wonder what others think.

So someone goes to the doctor's surgery complaining of tiredness. The doctor runs a battery of tests, checking he's not diabetic and so on. When he goes back, the doctor say that what showed up was hypothyroidism, and he prescribes some levothyroxine.

Now here's the thing, as I see it. If eating seaweed can impact the amount of that one needs, isn't it doing something of the same? Might not telling a patient to eat seaweed do instead? And even if it wouldn't, why wouldn't the doctor warn hypothyroid patients that eating seaweed could affect their dosage, like Dr. Wahls does?

It's kind of concerning the way some doctors just reach for a pharmaceutical that solves the immediate problem _and just leave it at that_.

> Now here's the thing, as I see it. If eating seaweed can impact the amount of that one needs, isn't it doing something of the same? Might not telling a patient to eat seaweed do instead? And even if it wouldn't, why wouldn't the doctor warn hypothyroid patients that eating seaweed could affect their dosage, like Dr. Wahls does?
>
>
>
> It's kind of concerning the way some doctors just reach for a pharmaceutical that solves the immediate problem _and just leave it at that_.

Agreed. My gut tells me this is precisely WHY I'm craving seaweed so much these days. My T3 is totally tanked. I just saw a new doc who ordered compounded T3 for me, but obviously if I can provide my body with what it needs to naturally make the hormone for me I'd much rather go that route.

### Michael — 2012-10-02T21:11:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-sea-veggies.4039/post-55904>

Thanks.

> **BJK77;57107 wrote:**
>
> Agreed. My gut tells me this is precisely WHY I'm craving seaweed so much these days. My T3 is totally tanked. I just saw a new doc who ordered compounded T3 for me, but obviously if I can provide my body with what it needs to naturally make the hormone for me I'd much rather go that route.
>
>
> Ho, hum ... how many of us? I was put on levothyroxine a few years ago. There seem to be ever so many people on it.
>
>
>
> That's interesting in itself. I think that guy who says "The message of this show is that human beings are not broken by default" has it right. So why do so many need thyroid medication? Why are all these thyroids breaking? Does your G.P. ever wonder as he fills out the prescription?
>
>
>
> The partners at my local practice seem quite good, prepared to take time, ready to talk to you, ready to actually think about some problem you bring them. And they're hard-working and have a high level of education ... and yet ...
>
>
>
> They just seem to look in their toolbox and rummage for a pharmaceutical. I'm inclined to wonder if doctors have an almost "magical" view of things. Probably not; probably that's just how it seems. But it's as if they think, "Ah, that's the problem! Here's the spell (pill) for that. Next please!" In reality much of the time they're handing out stuff that doesn't have a specific effect (as a spell would be supposed by primitive people to have), but a _handful of effects, one desirable (perhaps), others probably not. There's really no such thing as a "side-effect" -- that's_ really a "magical" way of thinking. There are only effects. And at the end of the day -- unoriginal thought here -- much of the time what they're offering only targets symptoms.
>
>
>
> My doctor, when putting me on levothyroxine, never mentioned diet to me. I wonder now: "Shouldn't he have warned me about goitrogens?" I also thought when I happened to learn that some people with thyroid conditions have problems converting beta-carotene to vitamin A -- they can even go orangey -- "Shouldn't he have told me to make sure I ate some foods with pre-formed vitamin A in them, like liver?"
>
>
>
> And, as I say, I think my doctors are pretty good. How about the one that had charge of this lady?
>
>
>
> He was still gathering information. But he did look at the list of medications, and as he did, his face grew hard and angry. He spoke crisply though, and kept his voice neutral: Okay. So she’s on these and these? Two pages of drugs?

One drug that gives someone a nasty side-effect, then another to target the side-effect, and so on in a cascade.

And at the bottom the problem is dietary anyway. Horrific and shocking!

[http://www.meandmydiabetes.com/2011/02/24/medicine-for-magdalena-checkup-with-ron-rosedale/](http://http://www.meandmydiabetes.com/2011/02/24/medicine-for-magdalena-checkup-with-ron-rosedale/)

How many prescribed drugs was Ozzie Osbourne on when he fell off that motorized quadricycle thing? Doctors said afterwards it was surprising he could function at all, even sit upright.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-10-02T23:55:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-sea-veggies.4039/post-55962>

I also thought when I happened to learn that some people with thyroid conditions have problems converting beta-carotene to vitamin A -- they can even go orangey -- "Shouldn't he have told me to make sure I ate some foods with pre-formed vitamin A in them, like liver?"
>
