### Jonathin — 2012-10-11T19:18:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diy-resveratrol.4128/post-57619>

Hi Caroline,

Nutritional cultures using EM (effective or efficient microorganisms) are minimally made by combining 5% liquid culture with 5% blackstrap molasses (for the sugars and diverse nutrients) and 90% pure water. This is done in a manner similar to wine so as to exclude oxygen. pH should drop to 3.6 or below. Usually this is done at room temperature. After about 2 weeks, the acidic liquid can be consumed or used in a number of other ways. A more sophisticated brewing would use more nutrient-rich ingredients such as certain berry and vegetable juices, seaweeds, herbs, etc to create a much more nutritious culture. Also the temperature is maintained at around 100 degres. Fermentation time is at least several months. I would also suggest using the EM cultures with that contain higher amounts of the PNSB organisms. The variations are many and there is no set recipe. The only thing that I am currently wondering about is the possibility of significant determintial compounds being formed during the process. It is probably similar to other home fermentation products like sourkraut, kimchi, etc.

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-10-12T02:37:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diy-resveratrol.4128/post-57680>

What is PNSB? How do you know you have more of these organisms? Are these the PNSB you are talking about:

[http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Purple\_bacteria](http://http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Purple_bacteria)

Where do you get your EM culture? Are EM cultures very different from one another?

### freesia — 2012-10-12T03:21:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diy-resveratrol.4128/post-57684>

Hi Caroline,

They do vary to some extent - local versions of EM are made in many countries. They should all contain purple non sulfur bacteria. The pnsb in my local starter comes from rock pools.

Teraganix makes EM in the USA, plus another company - I can't remember their name just now.

EM also contains yeasts and lactic bacteria, some versions have soil bacteria and actinomycetes. It's probably not a lot different to Kimchi made traditionally in clay pots buried in the ground....but in the USA at least not all these types of bacteria are sold intended for human consumption. I don't know if there's an official guide to using EM to ferment foodstuffs. I have seen a few recipes on the www. In the past I have corresponded with someone who helped develop a process where EM fermentation is used to improve the amino acid profile of vegetarian "protein supplements". The end result seems impressive.

I've played around with pnsb for a couple of years. They fascinate me! When the light cycles get long enough - as happened where I am in the Southern hemisphere about 3 weeks ago - they change metabolism and start harvesting light as their energy source and they glow red! The pnsb survive freezing and very high temperatures...and I think there's every chance we evolved with them.

If you're eating shellfish (frozen, cooked, raw...or whatever) you're possibly already eating some pnsb

I'm sure Jonathin can help with the name of the USA supplier I've forgotten.

Here are some of my pnsb friends...enjoying the sun!

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/59005=510-pnsb2.jpg)

PS you know you have more of the pnsb organisms by culturing them and seeing if you can get them to change metabolism and glow. You can also get them to glow under lights....if you do that don't forget to wear your blue blockers

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-10-13T22:51:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diy-resveratrol.4128/post-57994>

Thank you freesia.

That helped a lot. Are you using EM mostly in your garden? I have so many questions and I don't want to overload anyone. Do you have any books you would recommend for a beginner?

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-10-15T02:02:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diy-resveratrol.4128/post-58218>

Hi Friends,

Here is my second go at DIY poor man's resveratrol. These is the spent grapes, seeds and stems from traditional red wine making. The whole grape including the stems are put in a big vat and left to naturally ferment. Nothing is added. The nature yeast and bacteria found on the grapes start the fermentation process. After about a week the grapes and stems are taken out of the vat and put in a wine press. The grapes and stems are pressed until all the fluids come out. The more pressing the darker the wine. Here are the two pictures:

1\. This is the stems removed from the grape skins and seeds. (I will feed these stems to the hogs.)

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/59698=512-grape-stems-press.jpg)

2\. This is the grape skins and seeds which I will dehydrated.

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/59698=513-grape-skin-seeds-press.jpg)

After I dehydrate the grape skins I will likely remove the seeds and feed the seeds to the hogs. I will grind up the fermented skins into a **fermented DIY poor man's resveratrol**. I wonder what it will taste like? Most of the sugars will be gone.

### Jonathin — 2012-10-15T18:08:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diy-resveratrol.4128/post-58325>

Caroline - here is the other EM supplier:

[SCD Probiotics](http://http://www.scdprobiotics.com/SCD_Mother_Cultures_s/305.htm)

I would be very careful about molds getting started with your process if you aren't achieving sufficient acidity. Controlled facultative fermentation is what you want.

EM is great for pre-treating agricultural and food wastes. Treating soil is another matter except in unusual circumstances as it is an entirely different environment - 10,000's of different species and about 98% oxygen-consuming types.

### janagram — 2012-10-15T19:31:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diy-resveratrol.4128/post-58345>

[http://www.google.com/search?q=grape+seed+extract&hl=en&client=safari&rls=en&prmd=imvns&tbm=isch&tbo=u&source=univ&sa=X&ei=5Dl8UIndFtDriQKasIGYCQ&ved=0CFgQsAQ&biw=1236&bih=635](http://http://www.google.com/search?q=grape+seed+extract&hl=en&client=safari&rls=en&prmd=imvns&tbm=isch&tbo=u&source=univ&sa=X&ei=5Dl8UIndFtDriQKasIGYCQ&ved=0CFgQsAQ&biw=1236&bih=635)

# Thread: The Bog People
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-bog-people.4130/>
**Subforum:** Factor X
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Patty Cakes — 2012-10-05T01:01:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-bog-people.4130/post-56350>

_When the 3-year-old died, her parents placed her favorite toys in her arms, wrapped her in fabric woven from fibers of native plants, and buried her body in the soft, muck bottom of a small pond. Some 7,000 years later, when a young archaeologist uncovered her tiny remains, the toys--a wooden pestle-shaped object and the carapace of a small turtle--were still cradled in her arms. Most remarkable was the state of preservation of the child's bones and her toys, and the remains of some 167 other individuals and numerous artifacts found in that small pond in Windover Farms subdivision. The pond is about one mile southeast of the intersection of Highway 50 and I-95 and just outside the Titusville city limits where, today, a child's favorite toy may be a model of the space shuttle.

_

One of those, a female about 35 years of age at death, was buried face down and still had remnants of her last meal in her stomach--**fish scales and bones**, seeds from grasses and berries, and bits of nuts. There were more than 3,000 elderberry seeds in her stomach. Elderberry extract has been found to be beneficial in the treatment of some viral infections, but we have no way of knowing if this woman had eaten the berries as a treatment, or if she merely liked elderberries and possibly died of acute indigestion from eating so many._

A most significant find came only weeks into the project when one of the project directors found a lump of slippery, dark brown material inside a skull. There was cautious speculation that it might be preserved brain tissue, but common sense said that would not be possible--that any tissue would have dissipated into the black peat thousands of years ago. Laboratory tests proved however, that cautious speculation had become reality. The material was, indeed, human brain tissue._

[http://www.nbbd.com/godo/history/windover/](http://http://www.nbbd.com/godo/history/windover/)

### SeaHorse — 2012-10-05T02:33:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-bog-people.4130/post-56370>

Just finished the article...so interesting! Thanks for posting it. Interesting that many of the remains showed signs of arthritis.

### fitness@home — 2012-10-05T16:12:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-bog-people.4130/post-56469>

This is a very interesting article. Thanks for sharing!

As a child, I went through a phase of wanting to be an archeologist when I grew up

### bigknitwit — 2012-10-05T17:28:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-bog-people.4130/post-56481>

Fantastic read - thanks!

# Thread: Lab help Please!!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lab-help-please.4131/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Meg — 2012-10-05T01:12:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lab-help-please.4131/post-56352>

Just got labs back - the ranges are standard ranges. I am a 46 year old female.

Insulin Free

### KiwiLauren — 2012-10-05T02:49:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lab-help-please.4131/post-56375>

Meg, if I'm reading this correctly, you are low on E2, but not low on E overall (which means you are most likely making E3, the 'worst' kind of E... couldn't know for sure unless you got tested for E1 and E3). You are also very low on P adn DHEA. (I'm assuming you are not menopausal and that you tested around day 20/21 of your cycle... if not, you probably need to re-test at teh right time to get accurate P readings). Vitamin D is also low, especially coming out of summer. Your TSH is very high and you're not converting T4 to T3. Hopefully Armour will help with that. TSH can definitely fluctuate with environment. You don't seem to have an hsCRP, just a CRP (which has been called 'useless' in the past, though at least your CRP is trending down... I'd try to get an hsCRP, though, to better tell about inflammation because I don't think the CRP can give you that info).

You have some thyroid antibodies, so perhaps re-testing over the next 18 months a couple more times would be wise.

If you've tested positive for candida, the general consensus is to take big action to treat it. Which means things like colloidal silver, oregano oil or antibiotics. I wonder if you should re-test in a month and if you still show positive for candida, consider taking more action. Others have better info on this than I do.

I am clueless about some of the other labs.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-10-05T03:06:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lab-help-please.4131/post-56381>

If your adrenals are upside down you probably need to work on your circadian rhythms and keep it dark after sunset. If you can reverse the morning and evening results on the ASI you will probably be better able to handle NDT. Maybe you could try another brand of NDT instead of Armour. Many people have reported having issues with that brand. I personally like NP Thyroid based on my own results.

I didn't see a reverse T3 lab. Based on the other results you are not converting the T4 properly and it is most likely going to rT3. Also this would make sense with bad adrenals as they are often the cause of rT3. Adrenals should be treated ASAP as they hinder the progression of thyroid treatment IMO.

You also may have pregnenolone steal going on. Your body is probably shunting the preg to cortisol and not down the line to make other hormones.

Your potassium may be a little low in balance with the sodium. Something to ask your doc about as I am not very familiar with those labs.

Overall, it looks like adrenal work (darkness at night, sun in morning), hormonal balancing (bioidenticals, NDT or T3), and gut (Epi-paleo, EPCOTx) are the top three things to work on.

### Meg — 2012-10-05T14:31:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lab-help-please.4131/post-56449>

KiwiLauren - Thanks so much for replying. I am INTO menopause- one of the tests (called FSH) tells where you are in the process and I show that I am finished. I'm 46 and I blew thru it like a tornado in less than three years and I am still trying to catch my breath. My CRP doesn't have an HS with it so I'm guessing it's the worthless test?

### Meg — 2012-10-05T14:48:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lab-help-please.4131/post-56453>

Pattycakes- Thanks for taking the time to reply. I am finding that adrenals are quite tricky to heal and yet they tie into so many other important factors in getting your body right. JK said CT really helps adrenals so I am working on my CT habits - as well as so many other of my habits that I had no idea were "less than optimal".

If I may ask what do you like about NP over Armour? I have been on Armour in the past and it does me fine for a while then it starts to make me feel nausous exhausted and slightly depressed. In STTM book it says it can be because of adrenals but I don't understand why its fine for awhile - I guess are adrenals flucuate? I wonder if a different brand might be worth a try. I do know I should get my RT3 checked first.

Again, thanks for the help. I do a lot of reading on the forum and you always seem to have well timed wise word to offer people.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-10-05T18:30:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lab-help-please.4131/post-56490>

> **Meg;57713 wrote:** Pattycakes- Thanks for taking the time to reply. I am finding that adrenals are quite tricky to heal and yet they tie into so many other important factors in getting your body right. JK said CT really helps adrenals so I am working on my CT habits - as well as so many other of my habits that I had no idea were "less than optimal".
>
>
>
> If I may ask what do you like about NP over Armour? I have been on Armour in the past and it does me fine for a while then it starts to make me feel nausous exhausted and slightly depressed. In STTM book it says it can be because of adrenals but I don't understand why its fine for awhile - I guess are adrenals flucuate? I wonder if a different brand might be worth a try. I do know I should get my RT3 checked first.
>
> Again, thanks for the help. I do a lot of reading on the forum and you always seem to have well timed wise word to offer people.

One of my issues with Armour is I started on the OLD OLD formula then they reformulated and the new didn't have the same punch. They started using different fillers and the fillers I think were blocking the absorption for me. I know they have made some tweeks since then but I haven't gone back. I've also taken Naturethroid and Thyroid-S from Thailand and they both were fine. The NP seems to give the greatest punch for the smallest dosage. Everyone is different and has to find what works for them. Maybe your body went hypo after a short time on the Armour....could be something like fillers and also could be that you needed a raise in the dosage. I started out at 15mg and went all the way up to 150mg to get my T3 in the high end of the range. This is where you need a good doc and lots of testing. I used the guidance of the STTM book as well to help raise and it worked fabulously. The one thing about it is that it is not a whole body approach so keep up the epi-paleo eating habits, CT, etc which will help your thyroid, adrenals and more!

But the challenge for you could be your adrenals are so weak they will not allow much of a thyroid med in at all. CTing is not very fun for those of us with low FT3s. Just grin and bear it and sooner of later you will associate it as part of everyday life. It's an easy and cheap way to help fix the adrenals!

# Thread: Westonaprice.org an attack site...?

**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 12

### Raiken3712 — 2012-10-05T08:45:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/westonaprice-org-an-attack-site.4134/post-56422>

I keep getting this warning from my browser that westonaprice.org is an attack site. Some google thing says supposedly that its been flagged..as downloading trojans etc...etc.. to people's computers. I've never gotten anything that I can tell...My virus scans show nothing and I visit there quite a bit at times.

Is this part of a vegan/vegetarian cyber attack against one of their biggest foes or something...do they really have malware...that perhaps was put on their site by someone else...or maybe vegan/vegetarians are getting convincing google or whoever makes the attack page stuff to say its an attack site..

Really curious about this though. Has anyone else noticed this?

### Michael — 2012-10-05T09:05:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/westonaprice-org-an-attack-site.4134/post-56424>

> **Raiken3712;57680 wrote:**
>
> Is this part of a vegan/vegetarian cyber attack against one of their biggest foes or something
>
>
> Unlikely, I'd think. I've seen it, and I'm pretty sure it was the Google warning. I'm using Safari on a Mac here and it downloads "safe browsing data" from Google. I expect current Windows machines have something similar, although the data would probably come from Microsoft itself in that case.
>
>
>
> Not that someone _might not plant something on a site that creates a false pop-up that looks like something else (in this case a Google safe-browsing warning). But it seems unlikely. (I guess one could verify that by finding where the Google data is switched on in the browser's properties and temporarily turning it off - but that would be risky, if it turned out not_ to be a false positive.) Also, it has been happening for some time, so if it were cyber-crime surely the site owners would have shut the site down and cleaned it up by now.
>
>
>
> I should think it was a false positive - maybe something like the site using scripts that were similar to ones used for bad purposes could trigger something like that.
>
>
>
> ...do they really have malware...

I suppose it's a possibility. I'd have thought they'd have cleaned it up by now, if they had. The warning has been popping up for quite some time. But then I'd have thought they would have sorted out a false positive by now.

I guess you can't open the site to find a contact address to ask them what's going on.

Are you on Twitter? Maybe send Chris Masterjohn a question: he'd probably know what's going on.

### Raiken3712 — 2012-10-05T09:16:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/westonaprice-org-an-attack-site.4134/post-56425>

Yeah, I'm on twitter. I've heard of Chris Masterjohn but who is he exactly in relation to the Weston A. Price Foundation? One of the directors or something? I actually ignore the warning and go the site by copy and pasting the address in the warning. There might be a contact form on there or something but haven't found it.

I'll see about using Twitter.

I'm curious why this keeps popping up and why its not fixed. I'm thinking its a false positive as I would think I'd be infected with Trojans or something otherwise and I'm not. At least I'm fairly sure I'm not.

### Michael — 2012-10-05T10:41:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/westonaprice-org-an-attack-site.4134/post-56430>

> **Raiken3712;57683 wrote:** Yeah, I'm on twitter. I've heard of Chris Masterjohn but who is he exactly in relation to the Weston A. Price Foundation? One of the directors or something?
>
>
> I don't know whether he's a director, but he's associated with them. I believe some vegans had a consipracy theory along the lines that he didn't actually exist but was a conglomerate of mad professors. I guess that might illustrate that you're right to think these people have a bee in their bonnet about the WAPF. Vegans involved in cyber-attack, though? I doubt they'd have the energy: too busy trying to stay awake.
>
>
>
> Anyway, Chris seems to be a real person. He is one of two people whio regularly post at the WAPF blog.
>
>
>
> Last time the site was doing unexpected things - that time because they were re-organizing it - he seemed to know about and tweeted a couple of comments to people who asked.
>
>
>
> I'm curious why this keeps popping up and why its not fixed. I'm thinking its a false positive as I would think I'd be infected with Trojans or something otherwise and I'm not. At least I'm fairly sure I'm not.

I think false positive is the most likely explanation.

### Shijin13 — 2012-10-05T13:43:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/westonaprice-org-an-attack-site.4134/post-56444>

IME when this happens it usually means the site your going to was targeted and googles data collecting has identified attacks coming through the site I've seen this happen on horse racing sites & they are usually targeted by PETA & other such vegan/save the animals groups.

### Michael — 2012-10-05T14:09:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/westonaprice-org-an-attack-site.4134/post-56447>

IIRC either Symantec or McAffee has an online "site analyzer". You type in a URL and it'll tell you what, if anything, they think is wrong with a site.

If the Google alert is \*not\* a false positive and the WAPF has poor security such that someone has got in and has put malware there - either because they disapprove of the WAPF's policies or just because it's available space to use - then best not go there.

But it may just be error on Google's part.

Whatever's going it, it's been going on for sone time - days certainly, possibly a few weeks.

### Michael — 2012-10-05T14:23:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/westonaprice-org-an-attack-site.4134/post-56448>

Hang on ... there's a link on Google's blocking-page, giving a link to more information:

[http://www.google.com/safebrowsing/diagnostic?site=http://www.westonaprice.org/&hl=en](http://http://www.google.com/safebrowsing/diagnostic?site=http://www.westonaprice.org/&hl=en)

Reading that, my guess would be that they have poor security, and someone's just walked in and used it as a jumping-off point for infecting people - which, of course, has a commercial value. It's probably irrelevant that it happens to be the WAPF. People would put this stuff wherever they could get in.

### Kiera2 — 2012-10-06T08:14:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/westonaprice-org-an-attack-site.4134/post-56573>

A couple of weeks ago, Sally Fallon's email was hacked. People were getting requests for money, purporting to be from Sally. The email claimed that she was on holidays, had lost her passport and wallet, and needed help. There was someone else in WAPF whose email was hacked too, with the same message going out. I assume their site and email are related.

### Michael — 2012-10-06T09:04:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/westonaprice-org-an-attack-site.4134/post-56574>

Could be, couldn't it? They obtained the credentials in some way while they had access. Then later they tried to exploit them. One would have thought the first thing everyone would be told after a security breach had been discovered was: "Change your passwords".

I'd do that even if the security people didn't tell me! Less soaked grains and more fish needed.

With celebrities the papers always talk of email accounts being "hacked" but it's usually not. No exploit needed. This might be, say, a model or a footballer with a lurid lifestyle and they have an account at somewhere like Yahoo. Well, their emails have commercial value. So someone goes to the email-provider websiite, clicks "I have lost my password" and answers the "security question". That's usually something like "mother's maiden name" or "name of your first school". With celebrities all that information is available online. With any of us why answer such a question "truthfully"? Fill the box up with a string of random characters, write that down and lock it away.

I "blame sally". LOL

\[video\]\[video=youtube\_share;o1mhN6e93Z4\]http://www.youtube.com/watch?feature=player\_embedded&v=o1mhN6e93Z4\[/video\]\[/video\]

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-10-07T01:07:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/westonaprice-org-an-attack-site.4134/post-56644>

Hi Friends,

I am the **Weston A Price Foundation leader for Kamloops, BC, Canada. I have found out from the WAPF leader's board the the organization has been suffering from a series of attacks from hackers. **If you see the warning do not enter the site because hackers can have inserted code that could infect your computer.** Come back later. The people at the WAPF are working as quickly as they can to solve the problem. It wont be long. It just the series of attacks are happening so fast, it must be very frustrating for them.

The WAPF website is an excellent source of free information about alternatives to standard medical treatments and a great source of information about nourishing traditional diets.** I guess some people -- the hackers -- don't like what they have to say.

That said, better security is something they will have to do. From the WAPF board postings, people are really confused about what is happening and why. People have ideas but no one has been able to stop it from happening.

### kathylu — 2012-10-07T03:02:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/westonaprice-org-an-attack-site.4134/post-56658>

I'm going to Sally's farm tomorrow to pick up my pastured pork order. She lives about 15 miles from me. I'll ask what the scoop is.

### Michael — 2012-10-07T08:52:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/westonaprice-org-an-attack-site.4134/post-56682>

I don't think it's ... er ... healthy to get into a mindset where one immediately attrributes what is, after all, common enough opportunistic illegal activity to disagreement with one's opinions.

If you live in a bad area, and you leave the door open ... well, don't be surprised if someone walks in. (It should go without saying that the Internet is a bad area.)

# Thread: Ask Jack

**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions

### Kiera2 — 2012-10-06T01:49:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ask-jack.4143/post-56548>

With Jack deciding to be more strict with the rules, is there a way to allow _only_ Jack to respond to posts?

If people are using 'new posts' to read the forum, it's easy for their enthusiasm to help someone to override their noticing that they shouldn't post there. It can be very frustrating for the original poster, and I'm sure it must be frustrating for Jack to wade through posts, only to have to close them.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-10-06T02:24:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ask-jack.4143/post-56549>

+1 that's a great idea. I posted (just once) accidentally there and immediately realised it, apologised and asked for my comment to be deleted. But the thread was closed instead. I felt terrible but once I'd posted I couldn't do anything about it. Yet the person who asked the question didn't get a response because of my error.

### tellmisty — 2012-10-06T22:48:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ask-jack.4143/post-56624>

I'm going to appoint a couple of moderators in the new forums, which I think will be helpful for stuff like this.  Patience!

# Thread: Austrian notions of public health
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/austrian-notions-of-public-health.4149/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Michael — 2012-10-06T12:17:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/austrian-notions-of-public-health.4149/post-56575>

> Fortifying bread sold to the general public with folic acid could reduce the number of birth defects by up to 60 per cent, policymakers convening in Austria for the Gastein Health Forum have been told.
>
>
>
> Adding the acid to flour has been shown to substantially reduce the prevalence of neural tube defects such as spina bifida, says Scott Montgomery, Director of the Flour Fortification Initiative. ...
>
>
> I don't think I can be bothered to google him ...
>
>
>
>
> > Concerns about mandatory fortification include worries that it may lead to excess consumption of folic acid, that ... may cause cancer ..
> >
> >
> >
> > But Dr William Dietz, a former director of the Division of Nutrition art the US Center for Disease Control and Prevention says these concerns are unfounded: \`\`Folic acid fortification of flour carries few risks and has substantial health benefits," he will tell the conference.
>
>
>
> So that's all right then.
>
>
>
> Oh, we're not finished yet:
>
>
>
> Folic acid is not the only compound that could be used for fortification, vital supplies of iron and iodine could be given through the same route too.
>
>
>
> There are significant levels of anemia among women, especially pregnant women, where **iron deficiency can be an important contributor. And in several European countries, there are schoolchildren with mild or moderate iodine deficiency**. The conference will hear that both groups could benefit from fortification.

Hang on a moment ... have I got this right?

Public health advice in the U.S. and in most European countries is to consume large amounts of bread, a food which policymakers _know_ not to be delivering the nutrition people need, and their answer to that is to "fortify" the bread?

Furthermore, this will be done with compounds that don't deliver the nutrients in the form in which they would occur in foods, and will deliver them in the absence of possibly necessary co-factors.

Curioser and curioser. I feel like a small boy unable not to see that the Emperor has no clothes.

[http://austrianindependent.com/news/General\_News/2012-10-04/12328/Bread\_addiditive\_costing\_a\_penny\_a\_year\_could\_reduce\_birth\_defects\_by\_60\_percent](http://http://austrianindependent.com/news/General_News/2012-10-04/12328/Bread_addiditive_costing_a_penny_a_year_could_reduce_birth_defects_by_60_percent)


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Thread: Mess up my night routine, then can't sleep for hours
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mess-up-my-night-routine-then-cant-sleep-for-hours.4158/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### bigknitwit — 2012-10-07T07:11:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mess-up-my-night-routine-then-cant-sleep-for-hours.4158/post-56680>

This is so exasperating for me. If I do anything different in the evenings than usual, I can't fall asleep until after midnight (hence why I'm up). It's as if sleep completely breaks, and it really doesn't take much. I had to stay up to pick up dd from gymnastics at 9pm this evening (I kept my blue blockers on). I got home at 9:15 and went straight to bed and just laid awake. Usually I put my younger dd to bed around 8ish, and just stay upstairs relaxing and quiet, and until I fall asleep around 9 (yes, I really have no life). This problem has gone on for years. My sleep schedule is very inflexible! Once I do finally fall asleep on these nights (sometimes not until 2am), the sleep is terrible, and I wake up earlier than usual to start the (horrible) next day. What's happening? Does the change spike my cortisol and mess everything up? It's literally as if the ability to sleep gets broken.

I have tried all kinds of things to deal with this - phosphatidyl serine, melatonin, I even tried taking 5mg hydrocortisone, magnesium, calcium, etc, etc. I always stay in the bed, in the dark, to no avail. This evening I gave up and put on my blue blockers.

# Thread: Website Update:  Sunday, October 8, 2012
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/website-update-sunday-october-8-2012.4159/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### tellmisty — 2012-10-07T08:42:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/website-update-sunday-october-8-2012.4159/post-56681>

Hi everyone,

We are almost two weeks into the website transition, and thank God, things seem to be ironing out nicely!

**If you are a Gold Plus member (meaning you upgraded to Kruse Klub before the new site went live), you should have received an email from Team Kruse with instructions on booking your monthly consult. You will be getting an email for booking the next month's consult after your membership renews each month to help keep you on track. Remember, your consults do not accrue, so they must be used by you each month. Also, you may not "give" your consult to someone else ... the pricing for this membership level was priced to be used by one person.

**If you purchased a consult leading up to the new site changing over and you have not redeemed that consult, you will be getting an email within the next 48 hours with instructions on booking your consult. All consults purchased before the new site went live are good until the end of the year. Please keep that in mind as you're planning when to redeem the consult(s) you purchased.

\*\***All consults, please note\*\*:** Even if you've done a consult with Jack in the past, you will need to re-submit your Medical Disclaimer, Health History (we've created a worksheet to help you submit this information; there's a link to it in the email you will receive) and any labs. The reason for this is we are streamlining all data in this new system, and we want to make sure that your information is tied to your membership record in some kind of permanent way for tracking and delivery of your consult. Thanks for understanding ... there are thousands of you and we want to make sure we're tracking well!

If you purchased an email lab consult,** you will be sent an email with instructions within the next 48 hours. Please follow the instructions for redeeming your email lab consult ... you have until the end of the year.

If you have purchased an email lab consult or a regular 30-min or one-hour consult with Dr. Kruse and you have not received an email with instructions by Wednesday, October 10, [please open a ticket](http://http://jackkruse.zendesk.com) so we can double-check our records and straighten things out for you.

If your consults are set up in an account separate from your membership account,** please open a ticket and let us know the email addresses for the multiple accounts we have for you, and specify which email address you want to be on your primary account; we'll get things consolidated for you so that you don't have to login to multiple accounts to manage your webinars, consults, etc.

If you are wanting to buy a past webinar, either because you want a webinar older than the last three months (if you're a Silver Klub member), or you are not a member but want to buy a webinar, [keep an eye on the website's store](http://http://jackkruse.com/shop/). Darren is loading up all webinars so you can purchase them for immediate download from the site.

Thanks so much for your patience as we are transitioning everything ... we will work hard this week to iron out any bugs that come up via the consult registration process as we've done with the other issues, and I'm confident we'll find our way to a good place. We are also going to be transitioning the forums this week ... eeek. It's going to be sticky, so be patient! I'm already saying my Hail Marys for this one...

Hope you're all having a wonderful weekend!

Misty


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Looks like Dr. K is right again (ɷ3s this time)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looks-like-dr-k-is-right-again-%C9%B73s-this-time.4166/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Michael — 2012-10-07T17:33:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looks-like-dr-k-is-right-again-%C9%B73s-this-time.4166/post-56719>

Here's the takeaway:

> For people conscious about their heart health, a new study suggests it may be best to eat fish instead of taking individual omega-3 fatty acids in supplement form. ...
>
>
>
>
> It seems that:
>
>
>
>
> > One recent study ... analyzed data from multiple past studies and found **no link** between omega-3 supplements and overall death rates
>
>
>
> However, eating fish itself does look to be lowering people's risk:
>
>
>
> "Our findings showed a lower risk of heart failure in men consuming any amount of fish per week," Dr. Luc DjoussÃ©, a professor at Harvard Medical School in Boston said in an email.

[http://www.reuters.com/article/2012/10/02/us-fish-heart-failure-idUKBRE89118020121002](http://http://www.reuters.com/article/2012/10/02/us-fish-heart-failure-idUKBRE89118020121002)

### Inger — 2012-10-07T18:34:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looks-like-dr-k-is-right-again-%C9%B73s-this-time.4166/post-56728>

Yes. Thanks Michael. You reminded me of something I recently read when searching for information on heavy metals and other poison in fish from seas in and the sea around Finland. It is quite polluted because of Russia.. So they tell pregnant women to eat certain fish not at all, or very seldom. Then I read a bit further and the governement had made some studies about the fishermen in Finland and their health. It come out they are very healthy, some of the healthiest! So the governement was kind of confused how that could be, them eating all this polluted fish. Many had quite large amounts of certain heavy meatals too in their body when reaching old age, but despite that, still very healthy..?! Looks like Jack have it right...

I have to say I feel it in my guts.. the way nature intended it is the way to go.. with Iodine, with DHA, with anything.

Modern human come up with strange stuff.

### Michael — 2012-10-07T19:38:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looks-like-dr-k-is-right-again-%C9%B73s-this-time.4166/post-56747>

Found this, too (from a couple of years ago) - on a similar theme, and complete with quotes from Professor Michael Crawford.

Crawford doesn't seem to be against the oils _per se_, but keen to point out that some studies seem to back them in some applications, in face of a more dismissive attitude by another academic, Tom Sanders, who also states that "Vegetarians, who generally have very low levels of DHA in their diet, also have low levels of heart disease".

(Not sure on that one. Why is CVD more common in Southern India, where the incidence of vegetarianism is quite high, than farther north, then?

[http://www.wjgnet.com/1949-8462/full/v4/i4/112.htm](http://http://www.wjgnet.com/1949-8462/full/v4/i4/112.htm) )

Crawford does seem to be talking in terms of eating fish rather than popping oil pills though.

> However, neuroscientists and nutritionists recently told a London conference that many Britons' health is at risk as they aren't getting enough omega-3 from fish.
>
>
>
> 'There's an epidemic of brain and mental disorders in the UK, such as ADHD, depression and cerebral palsy, which cost the Government Â£77 billion a year,' says Professor Michael Crawford of the Institute of Brain Chemistry at London Metropolitan University.
>
>
>
> 'The evidence shows the lack of omega-3 from fish in our diet is a major factor.'
>
>
>
>
> Crawford also says:
>
>
>
> 'I predicted \[in the 1970s\] that a lack of good fats was going to eventually show up as damage to the brain. That is what is happening.'

[http://www.dailymail.co.uk/health/article-1294151/Millions-swear-fish-oil-pills-experts-say-theyre-waste-money--whos-right.html](http://http://www.dailymail.co.uk/health/article-1294151/Millions-swear-fish-oil-pills-experts-say-theyre-waste-money--whos-right.html)

# Thread: My optimal alarm clock
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-alarm-clock.4173/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-10-07T21:50:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-alarm-clock.4173/post-56787>

So now that freezing nights are here, we turn off the heat and start chilling the house right before dark, and let it get super cold at night for sleep. The heater is programmed to come on at 6 a.m., and this temperature change signals me to wake I think.

I am going to program the heater to go on with the sun - as I imagine that is part of the natural signal to the body and getting that slight rise in temps is part of syncing to the world.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-10-07T21:52:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-alarm-clock.4173/post-56788>

I don't know about Colorado, but in winter where I live the sunrise temps are not any warmer than the overnight temps. It takes until midday before you get a slight rise in temp. It seems here that only the light signals morning.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-10-07T21:56:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-alarm-clock.4173/post-56791>

> **KiwiLauren;58090 wrote:** I don't know about Colorado, but in winter where I live the sunrise temps are not any warmer than the overnight temps. It takes until midday before you get a slight rise in temp. It seems here that only the light signals morning.

I am envisioning that for most of our history, that we were sleeping outside or in caves - and that sunshine comin up would bring a slight rise where it's shining? OR perhaps it coming up signals all the animals to wake and they start being noisier.

What I do know is that the heat wakes me within 10 mins of comin on

### Shijin13 — 2012-10-07T21:57:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-alarm-clock.4173/post-56792>

> **Cavemam;58089 wrote:** So now that freezing nights are here, we turn off the heat and start chilling the house right before dark, and let it get super cold at night for sleep. The heater is programmed to come on at 6 a.m., and this temperature change signals me to wake I think.
>
>
>
> I am going to program the heater to go on with the sun - as I imagine that is part of the natural signal to the body and getting that slight rise in temps is part of syncing to the world.

one question for you ...what are you doing for your pipes? we're going to embrace keeping it as cold as we can until it start drops to the mid/low 40s - as freezing pipes becomes a possibility here....

### ealachan — 2012-10-07T21:58:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-alarm-clock.4173/post-56794>

> **Cavemam;58093 wrote:** I am envisioning that for most of our history, that we were sleeping outside or in caves - and that sunshine comin up would bring a slight rise where it's shining? OR perhaps it coming up signals all the animals to wake and they start being noisier.
>
>
>
> What I do know is that the heat wakes me within 10 mins of comin on

Our furnace wakes me up too, but mostly because it's old and loud...lol

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-10-07T22:01:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-alarm-clock.4173/post-56795>

> **Shijin13;58094 wrote:** one question for you ...what are you doing for your pipes? we're going to embrace keeping it as cold as we can until it start drops to the mid/low 40s - as freezing pipes becomes a possibility here....

I have a newish energy efficient house with pipes on interior or buried - so I think the overnight isn't enough time for it. In fact, although vacant houses are "winterized" here out of habit, I have yet to see anybodies pipes actually have an issue. I think the older the pipes, the bigger the issue. hehe if I were you I would worry about those 30K windows!!!!! A 2K plumbing bill is nothing compared to that! heheh

### Shijin13 — 2012-10-07T22:13:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-alarm-clock.4173/post-56801>

In fact, although vacant houses are "winterized" here out of habit, I have yet to see anybodies pipes actually have an issue. Yeah those windows are pricy. But b/c they are single panes they let in lots of cold air!!!

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/58103=502-ImageUploadedByTapatalk1349637188.810960.jpg)

### KiwiLauren — 2012-10-07T23:46:11+0300

# Thread: Marine Plasma
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/marine-plasma.4175/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Glen PDQ — 2012-10-07T22:38:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/marine-plasma.4175/post-56803>

Is there anything to the substance called Marine Plasma which has been used in France since since WWII? Supposedly there are spots in the ocean where massive planktonic blooms convert minerals in the saltwater to readily usable nutrients which match the profile in perfectly healthy human blood. The harvested water is filtered and has been used to treat sick infants, the elderly, and athletes. It appears there was a massive turnout at the inventors funeral in France with large crowds of mothers holding their infants raised up in their arms as a sort of tribute to the man.

This sounds like a possible "food as medicine". I am curious about it and would like to hear anything pro or con.

# Thread: The Journey of Mankind
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-journey-of-mankind.4183/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Michael — 2012-10-08T08:56:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-journey-of-mankind.4183/post-56869>

An interactive map from the Bradshaw Foundation, based on Professor Stephen Oppenheimer's work, showing the "journey of mankind".

You might have to turn off pop-up blockers to get the full content.

"Based on a synthesis of mtDNA and Y-chromosome evidence with archaeology, climatology and fossil study" the map tracks the routes and times of migration in the peopling of the world.

I'll note in passing that most of the movement seems to be along coasts or down river-valleys.

[http://www.bradshawfoundation.com/journey/](http://http://www.bradshawfoundation.com/journey/)

# Thread: Population Densities in Pre-Columbian North America

══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### laura — 2012-10-08T15:33:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epigenetics-course.4187/post-56880>

I like to take the **free** courses offered by top-rate university through coursera. They have an epigenetics course coming up that may be of interest to those who read this blog and forum. You can use this link to learn the details. [https://www.coursera.org/#course/epigenetics](http://https://www.coursera.org/#course/epigenetics)

The courses are great, usually don't require any additional, costly materials and allow you to study topics on your time.

# Thread: Sharks Threatened
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sharks-threatened.4193/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Michael — 2012-10-08T21:01:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sharks-threatened.4193/post-56932>

> DEEP-sea sharks, among the slowest growing and breeding of marine species, face being wiped out to meet demand for fish oil dietary supplements.
>
>
>
> Species such as the leafscale gulper shark, which can take 35 years to reach breeding age, and the kitefin shark, which has a similar life cycle, are being hunted down because their livers contain large quantities of the valuable oils. ...
>
>
> Not good news.
>
>
>
> Xavier Pastor, executive director of Oceana Europe, said ... "Vulnerable deep-sea sharks have become the new gold. They are targeted by fishing fleets for their liver oil for use in omega 3 dietary supplements, in cosmetics, and as industrial lubricants, and also for their fins and meat," he said. "These species are extremely vulnerable."

[http://www.theaustralian.com.au/news/world/diet-demands-threaten-sharks/story-fnb64oi6-1226490186469](http://http://www.theaustralian.com.au/news/world/diet-demands-threaten-sharks/story-fnb64oi6-1226490186469)

The story seems to have been syndicated from the _Sunday Times_, which, I think, is behind a paywall.

Here is Oceana's site:

[http://oceana.org/en/eu/home](http://http://oceana.org/en/eu/home)

I'm thinking that perhaps this is another reason to prefer fish over fish-oil - the conservation point of view. People are preferring to supplement with the oil, because they don't like the taste of fish or whatever. They need to man-up (or woman-up) and eat the whole fish, because the result of the demand for fish-oil is that these rare species, because their livers can account for a quarter of their bodyweight, have become targets for over-exploitation.

### JoeBranca — 2012-10-19T21:45:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sharks-threatened.4193/post-59224>

Check out this trailer, turning the Jaws meme on its head. Plus I'm biased towards anything Malick/Lubezki are involved with.

[http://www.devilsteethmovie.com/index.htm](http://http://www.devilsteethmovie.com/index.htm)

### KiwiLauren — 2012-10-20T00:37:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sharks-threatened.4193/post-59243>

I listened to a podcast interview with Sylvia Earle, oceanographer and aquanaut (often called Her Deepness). She talked lovingly about sharks. She said, from her point of view, when you see sharks in the ocean it means the ocean is in good health. But she says she rarely sees them anymore.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-10-21T01:34:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sharks-threatened.4193/post-59407>

> **KiwiLauren;60784 wrote:** I listened to a podcast interview with Sylvia Earle, oceanographer and aquanaut (often called Her Deepness). She talked lovingly about sharks. She said, from her point of view, when you see sharks in the ocean it means the ocean is in good health. But she says she rarely sees them anymore.

Kiwi - you are my kinda gal! I wish you were not around the world!

# Thread: lemon balm
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lemon-balm.4195/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### chocolate — 2012-10-08T22:21:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lemon-balm.4195/post-56939>

> [http://blog.lef.org/2012/10/manage-hyperthyroidism-naturally.html](http://http://blog.lef.org/2012/10/manage-hyperthyroidism-naturally.html)
>
> Lemon Balm Supports a Calm Mood
>
> One of the most troubling symptoms of hyperthyroidism is anxiety, which can make you feel irritable and restless. **Fortunately, research shows that lemon balm can help support a calm mood by inhibiting the breakdown of gamma-aminobutyric acid (GABA), a neurotransmitter which calms the nervous system.4
>
>
>
> In a clinical trial involving people with mild-to-moderate anxiety, participants were given lemon balm extract for 15 days. They were interviewed with the Free Rating Scale for Anxiety (FRSA), a tool which measures anxiety symptoms.
>
>
>
> The results of the study were significant. Participants felt less anxious and even slept better at night. Remarkably, over 95% of the participants responded to the treatment.4
>
>
>
> For this reason, you may want to consider supplementing with lemon balm to help with hyperthyroidism-inspired anxiety.
>
>
> [http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Melissa\_officinalis](http://http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Melissa_officinalis)
>
>
>
>
> > It is also used as an anxiolytic, mild sedative or calming agent. At least one study has found it to be effective at reducing stress, although the study's authors call for further research.\[10\] Lemon balm extract was identified as a potent inhibitor of GABA transaminase, which explains anxiolytic effects. The major compound responsible for GABA transaminase inhibition activity in lemon balm is rosmarinic acid.\[11\]
> >
> >
> >
> > Lemon balm and preparations thereof also have been shown to improve mood and mental performance. These effects are believed to involve muscarinic and nicotinic acetylcholine receptors.\[12\] Positive results have been achieved in a small clinical trial involving Alzheimer patients with mild to moderate symptoms.\[13\] Essential oils obtained from Melissa officinalis leaf showed high acetylcholinesterase and butyrylcholinesterase co-inhibitory activities.\[14\]
> >
> >
> >
> > Its antibacterial properties have also been demonstrated scientifically, although they are markedly weaker than those from a number of other plants studied.\[15\] The extract of lemon balm was also found to have exceptionally high antioxidant activity.\[16\]
> >
> >
> >
> > Lemon balm is mentioned in the scientific journal Endocrinology, where it is explained that Melissa officinalis exhibits antithyrotropic activity, inhibiting TSH from attaching to TSH receptors, hence making it of possible use in the treatment of Graves' disease or hyperthyroidism.\[17\]
>
>
>
> Ob-X, a mixture of three herbs, Morus alba, M. officinalis, and Artemisia capillaris, may help regulate obesity. Ob-X reduces body weight gain and visceral adipose tissue mass in genetically obese mice.\[22\]
>
>
>
> Recent research found a daily dose of the tea reduced oxidative stress status in radiology staff that were exposed to persistent low-dose radiation during work. After only 30 days of taking the tea daily researchers found Lemon balm tea resulted in a significant improvement in plasma levels of catalase, superoxide dismutase, and glutathione peroxidase and a marked reduction in plasma DNA damage, myeloperoxidase, and lipid peroxidation.\[23\]
>
> **
>
> Lemon balm was found to be effective in the amelioration of laboratory-induced stress in human subjects, producing "significantly increased self-ratings of calmness and reduced self-ratings of alertness." The authors further report a "significant increase in the speed of mathematical processing, with no reduction in accuracy" following the administration of a 300 mg dose.\[24\]
>
>
>
> Lemon balm is the main ingredient of Carmelite Water, which is still for sale in German pharmacies.\[25\]

### hope2conceive — 2012-10-10T23:16:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lemon-balm.4195/post-57426>

Lemon balm is the main ingredient of Carmelite Water, which is still for sale in German pharmacies.\[25\]

\-- Love it.....

# Thread: Sept Labs are BACK! Well... some of them
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sept-labs-are-back-well-some-of-them.4196/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 43
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Shijin13 — 2012-10-08T22:44:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sept-labs-are-back-well-some-of-them.4196/post-56946>

Got my Sept labs back - well most of them... the hormone labs from Genova haven't come in yet... so I'm running blood work for the hormones on Thursday

I'm on a 1/2 grain (30mg) of armour thyroid....

Thyroid (RANGE)

T4 1.01 ng/dL (.82-1.77)

TSH 4.53 uIU/mL (.450-4.5) (I knew this was happing...

Free T3 3.4pg/mL (2.0-4.4)

rT3 18.1 ng/dL (13.5-34.2)

T3:rT3 ratio 18.8

### KiwiLauren — 2012-10-08T23:12:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sept-labs-are-back-well-some-of-them.4196/post-56952>

Gretchen, with such high T3 but also elevated TSH, what does this mean? You've obviously been feeling hypo since you said you knew it was happening, but your T3 is over the range. What do you make of this?

### Shijin13 — 2012-10-08T23:32:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sept-labs-are-back-well-some-of-them.4196/post-56955>

My PCP thinks its the fact that the rT3 is getting low finally. But she's worried about the fact my TSH is off the charts... and the fact the T4 was low even w/the armour (which is a NTD T4/T3 combo)

a part of me wonders if the high T3 isn't just a result of me responding to summer carbs??? and we'll see it drop in the winter again... guess we'll see... I'd love to hear Patty's or Colleen's take on this...

I've known all along I've had thyroid issues... we're just now peeling the layers back so we can see what's actually going on... good thing is if I'm making T3 - that means I'm making it in the gut...so my gut must be on the road to optimal....and if I'm making T3...then I should be converting LDL -> pregnenolone -> progesterone (fingers crossed!)

### maggie — 2012-10-09T00:06:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sept-labs-are-back-well-some-of-them.4196/post-56961>

> **Shijin13;58257 wrote:** if I'm making T3 - that means I'm making it in the gut...so my gut must be on the road to optimal....and if I'm making T3...then I should be converting LDL -> pregnenolone -> progesterone (fingers crossed!)

Shijin, congrats on the improved labs! Must be good to see things moving in the right direction.

I wondered about your statement I quoted above. Could you unpack that a little? I don't understand why it means you're making T3 in the gut. Would like to understand. Thanks

### Shijin13 — 2012-10-09T00:15:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sept-labs-are-back-well-some-of-them.4196/post-56966>

T4 is converted in the Gut to T3... Jack mentioned this in the EPCOTx webinar.... so if your gut is bad - you're not going to have optimal hormone conversion... he also covers it in BG12 & CT7 (If I remember correctly)

you need K2 to help convert T4->T3, K2 is made in the gut. and its easiest to convert T4->T3 where K2 is most prevelant.

you need T3 + Vitamin A to convert LDL ->pregnenolone

If you in a stressed pathway your LDL->pregnenolone -> cortisol

If you in the optimal pathway your LDL->pregnenolone->progesterone and all other hormones...

I think I understand this - but I could have missed a piece and Jack will correct me if I'm off base... but these are all things he and I talked about during my consult (remember N=1, so this may not all apply to you)

### Patty Cakes — 2012-10-09T00:16:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sept-labs-are-back-well-some-of-them.4196/post-56968>

You are definitely getting T3 from somewhere. Also the range for T3 seems very narrow. It looks like no FT3 test, right? ou could finally be getting the T3 you need due to the clearing of the rT3. You are almost on the other side of that! Congrats!

Anyway my thoughts are not totally clear on this. The high TSH is your pituitary sending the signal that more thyroid hormone is needed. That usually corresponds to low readings on T3/4 labs. With your T3 being high and T4 at least being in range I am wondering if something is off in the pituitary. Does Cycloset do anything to that gland?

### Shijin13 — 2012-10-09T00:26:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sept-labs-are-back-well-some-of-them.4196/post-56974>

whoops that was a Free T3... need to fix that...

I need to go back and read again my cycloset stuff - but I've been feeling like my thyroid was a bit off, in July before I started the cycloset... I felt this way back in Jan... and I knew my TSH was out of the park... as I keep peeling back the layers and we fix something... my Pitutary wakes up and jars my thyroid to make more! only this time... its kinda starting to work...

### Patty Cakes — 2012-10-09T00:41:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sept-labs-are-back-well-some-of-them.4196/post-56976>

> **Shijin13;58276 wrote:** whoops that was a Free T3... need to fix that...

> I need to go back and read again my cycloset stuff - but I've been feeling like my thyroid was a bit off, in July before I started the cycloset... I felt this way back in Jan... and I knew my TSH was out of the park... as I keep peeling back the layers and we fix something... my Pitutary wakes up and jars my thyroid to make more! only this time... its kinda starting to work...

The part I don't get is that darn TSH. Why is the pituitary still saying you need more T hormone? Are you feeling hypo at all? I'm quite fascinated and would love to hear the explanation of this. Sure the TSH isn't that high because your T4 is in the low part of the range...or is that why? I'm sure the iodine is helping that FT3 result. Actually it looks like you are taking Cytomel. High FT3 and low range FT4.

### Shijin13 — 2012-10-09T00:51:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sept-labs-are-back-well-some-of-them.4196/post-56979>

> **Patty Cakes;58278 wrote:** The part I don't get is that darn TSH. Why is the pituitary still saying you need more T hormone? Are you feeling hypo at all? I'm quite fascinated and would love to hear the explanation of this. Sure the TSH isn't that high because your T4 is in the low part of the range...or is that why? I'm sure the iodine is helping that FT3 result. Actually it looks like you are taking Cytomel. High FT3 and low range FT4.

I do know my pitutary is a bit "out of sorts" my prolactin levels are below range, and I'm nursing (not that I haven't been trying to wean Havoc) still. and those levels explain why I needed domperidone to induce/support lactation w/both of my kids. W/ds I actually was able to stop taking it last December and still make milk while only having him nursing 3x/day - its kinda like my pitutary just isn't firing on all cylindars

I guess my question to you you is what are symptoms of hypo?

No weight loss (just stagnated) of recent I've been hot as hades, with crazy "hot flashes" but they're not peri-menopausal hot flashes, and my temp will be 97 when these hot flashes occur.

Today I'm actually cold - but then again its the first real cool day we've had - highs are in the mid 60s and its wet... I'm actually enjoying the cold feeling... been shivering all day...in a light cotton tshirt....

### KiwiLauren — 2012-10-09T00:55:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sept-labs-are-back-well-some-of-them.4196/post-56981>

Hypo would be feeling cold, cold hands and feet, sluggishness, weight gain/no weight loss. But with such a high T3 it's hard to imagine you being traditionally hypo. That TSH was what I didn't understand either. I have the same question: why would your pituitary be signalling the need for more T3 when it's already high? Perhaps getting hormone results will help explain things more?

One semi-related question, we know T3 is made in the gut. Where is RT3 made? Also in the gut? (I'm thinking that theoretically you could have a healthy gut and convert a lot of T4 to RT3... is that possible?)

### Patty Cakes — 2012-10-09T01:12:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sept-labs-are-back-well-some-of-them.4196/post-56988>

I know rT3 is made when adrenals are dysfunctioning or iron is very low and maybe some other things but those are the biggies. Also FT4 is converted to FT3 in the liver and a small amount in the gut. So I think rT3 is made in either one of those two places. Not a very scientific explanation. :-/

### steve@wity.net — 2012-10-09T10:21:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sept-labs-are-back-well-some-of-them.4196/post-57065>

### bigknitwit — 2012-10-09T15:32:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sept-labs-are-back-well-some-of-them.4196/post-57094>

How long before the labs did you take your dose of Armour? That would impact the FT3 on the labs...

Another thought: TSH takes some time to shift. So if you've changed something recently that's impacting your thyroid, your TSH may not reflect that just yet.

### Shijin13 — 2012-10-09T17:47:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sept-labs-are-back-well-some-of-them.4196/post-57102>

>
>
>
> Another thought: TSH takes some time to shift. So if you've changed something recently that's impacting your thyroid, your TSH may not reflect that just yet.

Took the armour at 5:30 am. Blood work was at 8:30

Felt the thyroid kick in in July after my 1st consult w/Jack and he told me to push the seafood, the longer I've been epipaleo tge more I've felt like it was off.

### bigknitwit — 2012-10-09T18:01:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sept-labs-are-back-well-some-of-them.4196/post-57104>

> **Shijin13;58404 wrote:** Took the armour at 5:30 am. Blood work was at 8:30
>
>
>
> Felt the thyroid kick in in July after my 1st consult w/Jack and he told me to push the seafood, the longer I've been epipaleo tge more I've felt like it was off.

Well, I'm no expert, but I'd say the T3 from your Armour dose at 5:30am was at about maximum concentration at the time of your blood draw. And then it would slowly recede throughout the next several hours. Is the T4 value you gave a free, or a total? Either way, after the am boost from the Armour, your body is now stuck with creating T3 from your (low) T4 levels. I'm guessing you need more Armour, dosed mid-afternoon.

Also, isn't 80% of the conversion of T4 to T3 done in the liver? I thought only 20% happened in the gut?

### Patty Cakes — 2012-10-09T18:02:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sept-labs-are-back-well-some-of-them.4196/post-57105>

I don't like to take the NDT before the blood test in the morning. I always wait until after the test. NOW that the range has been adjusted your FT3 is still a little low and show be at least 4.0. I felt like a rockstar when mine was at 4.2. Not hyper at all.

So you have a high TSH, slightly low FT3 and low FT4. This all seems to fit. You are still exhibiting being a little hypo in my eyes. But if things are kicking in you may want to wait another 3 months and see how things go. The 30mg Armour you are taking is really a tiny dose. I bet you could up it to 45 or 60 mg and be just fine. But, and I could be completely off base here, you want your thyroid to kick in and not rely on drugs so you don't want to take so much that you suppress your body's own ability to pump out that FT4 and FT3. Someone correct me if that last statement is completely off base.

### bigknitwit — 2012-10-09T18:03:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sept-labs-are-back-well-some-of-them.4196/post-57106>

Also, it would be interesting to compare these thyroid labs to your last set. How long have you been taking the Armour? Was your last set of thyroid labs "better?"

>
>

I'm inclined to go up slowly... but damn I'm hot here at work - feel like I'm burning up, and every one else is telling me its cold in the building (except 1 other person who agrees w/me that its freaking HOT!) while its a wet cold out side mid 50s/low 60s' w/high humidity - its a damp cold... I'm actually comfortable - even when my hands are cold, I feel like I'm cool.
>
>
>

whats funny is my prolactin levels have come up on cycloset (which its supposed to suppress and my milk supply hasn't dried up either!) so something's working at the pituitary level I think...

### Patty Cakes — 2012-10-10T02:00:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sept-labs-are-back-well-some-of-them.4196/post-57204>

> **Shijin13;58483 wrote:** what do your recent labs look like???
>
>
>
> I know my HOT isn' a typical peri/menopausal hot flash... I'm just freaking hot... I can't stand it if the temp is above 70, pretty much right now... ughhh.

My labs when I was on NDT were practically perfect (no meds taken that morning):

FT3-4.2 (2.0-4.4)

FT4-1.44 (.82-1.77)

TSH-.006

but.....I had a rT3 ratio of 16.8.

So I went on T3 to bring that ratio above 20. Really like this T3 only.

August labs (no meds taken that morning):

FT3 - 2.2 (2.0-4.4)

FT4 - 1.44 (.82-1.77)

TSH - didn't test

rT3 ratio - 44 whoo-hoo!!

Since t3 only is a fast acting and doesn't stay in the system much longer than 8 hours there was nothing left the morning of that testas is shown with the low reading. Compared to NDT which has the T4 (which is converting into the next day) and T3. Thinking maybe there is a seasonal connection but hard to tell with the T3 only. This is when I think it would be good to take the med about 2 hours before testing as I think that is when T3 peaks.

Because those NDT labs were so good I think my body likes NDT better. Contemplating going back...

### KiwiLauren — 2012-10-15T23:41:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sept-labs-are-back-well-some-of-them.4196/post-58404>

> **bigknitwit;59855 wrote:** My house is an hour of insanity before school each morning, and I'm not going anywhere (sometimes I drive them in my PJs!). You have my complete respect for being able to pull off what you do, daily!

BKW, I am a solo parent Monday-Friday of every week (my husband lost his local work after the earthquakes) and have been for over a year. You CAN do this. I find two things required to keep my morning going to task... one is to get up before the children. Even if that means having to set a non-optimal alarm clock  while my ASI panel crawls its way to optimal. The other is to keep a sense of humour. I tell my kids "I don't want to be a stressed-out-crazy-tantruming-mummy anymore" so here's what we all have to do. Their jobs are small, mine are huge. Once they are awake, I put on fun music, keep an eye on the clock and begin multi tasking. (Getting up early means I've already had my thyroid meds, been outside to sun-gaze and stretch, got dressed and even checked the forums.... thankfully with this lifestyle showering is no longer a necessity, which is a great time-saver!). Oh, and I literally say to myself every morning before I get out of bed, 'thanks for another day to be alive.' It helps.

### AKMan — 2012-10-16T00:47:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sept-labs-are-back-well-some-of-them.4196/post-58429>

> **bigknitwit;58396 wrote:** How long before the labs did you take your dose of Armour? That would impact the FT3 on the labs...
>
>
>
> Another thought: TSH takes some time to shift. So if you've changed something recently that's impacting your thyroid, your TSH may not reflect that just yet.

This makes a big difference! I pulled a fast one recently, I had two docs each put in a lab request for TSH, T4 and FT3. I took Armour 30mg at 6am, had first test at 8am and second at 2:30pm. Results were:

8am:

TSH - 3.05 (ref .34 - 4.82)

FT4 - 1.01 (.59 - 1.61NG/DL))

FT3 - 8.1 (3.1 - 7.7pmol/L)

2:30pm:

TSH - 3.00

FT4 - 1.00

FT3 - 5.1

I was told that all of T3 from Armour is out of your system in 8 hours. This kind of backs that up. It seems it would be better to skip your morning dose of NDT if you want to see what your own thyroid is producing and take your morning dose if you want to see what the NDT is providing.

### bigknitwit — 2012-10-16T01:26:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sept-labs-are-back-well-some-of-them.4196/post-58443>

Thanks! I am on my own in the mornings (my husband got a great new job in a not-so-great new location, so he leaves at 5:30am). We have it pretty well organized now. I help my younger 2 make their lunches/snacks for school the night before. The older 2 make their own lunch in the mornings, and seem to pull it off pretty well. I have some stuff available in the fridge (cut fruit, etc), that is easy for them to use. The boys empty the dishwasher, I get the meds out for thyroid kids, and I get started on cooking breakfast - fish for everyone! I can't believe that somehow they've all started eating it, everyday!

It's always the last minute stuff that gets crazy. But the insanity of getting everyone in the car and out the door is usually in good humour. In a few weeks we will "fall back" and gain an hour. This is the first year this will really help my household, because my kids are really tuned at getting up with the sun and are sleeping later now. I have a difficult time waking my 6 year old (something I've never really had to do with any of them, or even myself) to get ready for school.

# Thread: Garden help needed?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/garden-help-needed.4200/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-10-09T00:08:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/garden-help-needed.4200/post-56963>

Ok good people - this is my first time to grow anything. It's froze the last couple of nights. My basil is all black and droopy.

The parsley, stevia, tyme, oregeno and mints look ok as does the kale. The tomato plants look all wilted and blackish too.

What are you supposed to do in winter? I planted some cauliflower, kale and cabbages cause I thought they were winter stuff. . .but the sprinklers are turned off. . . do you water in the winter? What can I do with all my herbs that are still alive, anything I can do with the blackened basil? Is this a delicacy anywhere?

Thankfully I've harvested about 5 quarts of pesto already so I don't feel too cheated.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-10-09T00:16:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/garden-help-needed.4200/post-56967>
